turns.content:
With ~387 instructions, data starting at x3183 to x3282. But the earlier LD instructions at x3000 need to reach data at x3183 — that's an offset of +387, which exceeds 255! This is the core issue.

The proper solution for longer LC-3 programs is to interleave data constants near the subroutines that use them (which is what the reference snippets do — each subroutine has its own data declarations right after the `RET`). I've already done this for `SaveR7` variables, but I need to move **all** constants (NegX, NegC, etc.) close to the main dispatch loop.

Let me rewrite with a cleaner approach — put the command dispatch constants right at the top near the dispatch code:

tool_calls.name: write
tool_calls.arguments:
{"content": "; ============================================================
; LC-3 Stack-Based Calculator
; Commands: X C + * % @ neg D  and digit input
;
; Stack conventions:
;   R6 = stack pointer  (points to top element)
;   Empty: R6 == addr(StackBase)     Full: R6 == addr(StackMax)
;   PUSH: if full -> error; else R6--, M[R6]=R0
;   POP : if empty -> error; else R0=M[R6], R6++
;   R5=0 success  R5=1 failure
;
; Result range: [-999, 999]; else error + stack restored.
; ============================================================
        .ORIG   x3000

; ============================================================
; MAIN PROGRAM
; ============================================================
        LEA     R6,StackBase        ; init stack pointer (empty)

NewCommand
        LEA     R0,PromptMsg
        PUTS
        GETC
        OUT

Test
        LD      R1,NegX
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     ExitProg

        LD      R1,NegC
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpClear

        LD      R1,NegPlus
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpAdd

        LD      R1,NegMult
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpMult

        LD      R1,NegPct
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpMod

        LD      R1,NegAt
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpXOR

        LD      R1,NegN
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     ReadNeg

        LD      R1,NegD
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpDisplay

        LD      R1,NegMinus
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     PushValue

        ; Digit: '0' (x30) .. '9' (x39)
        LD      R1,NegZero
        ADD     R2,R0,R1
        BRn     NewCommand
        LD      R1,NegNine
        ADD     R2,R0,R1
        BRp     NewCommand
        BRnzp   PushValue

; --- Dispatch constants (kept close to Test for PC-offset reach) ---
NegX       .FILL  xFFA8
NegC       .FILL  xFFBD
NegPlus    .FILL  xFFD5
NegMult    .FILL  xFFD6
NegPct     .FILL  xFFDB
NegAt      .FILL  xFFC0
NegN       .FILL  xFF92
NegD       .FILL  xFFBC
NegMinus   .FILL  xFFD3
NegZero    .FILL  xFFD0
NegNine    .FILL  xFFC7

; ============================================================
; ReadNeg: consume 'e','g' to complete "neg"
; ============================================================
ReadNeg
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R1,NegE
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRnp    NewCommand
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R1,NegG
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRnp    NewCommand
        BRnzp   OpNeg

NegE       .FILL  xFF9B
NegG       .FILL  xFF99

ExitProg
        HALT

; ============================================================
; OpClear
; ============================================================
OpClear
        LEA     R6,StackBase
        BRnzp   NewCommand

; ============================================================
; OpAdd  (a=top, b=second;  push b+a)
; ============================================================
OpAdd
        ST      R7,OpAddR7
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpAddExit
        ADD     R1,R0,#0
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpAddR1
        ADD     R0,R0,R1
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpAddR2
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,OpAddR7
        BRnzp   NewCommand
OpAddR2
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpAddR1
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpAddExit
        LD      R7,OpAddR7
        BRnzp   NewCommand
OpAddR7   .FILL  x0000

; ============================================================
; OpMult  (a=top, b=second;  push b*a)
; Uses repeated addition; tracks sign via toggle flag R3
; ============================================================
OpMult
        ST      R7,OpMultR7
        AND     R3,R3,#0
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpMultExit
        ADD     R1,R0,#0            ; R1 = a
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpMultR1
        ; Determine sign from a
        ADD     R1,R1,#0
        BRzp    MultAPos
        NOT     R3,R3
        AND     R3,R3,#1            ; toggle sign flag
        NOT     R1,R1
        ADD     R1,R1,#1            ; R1 = |a|
MultAPos
        ; Determine sign from b (in R0)
        ADD     R2,R0,#0
        BRzp    MultBPos
        NOT     R3,R3
        AND     R3,R3,#1
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1            ; R2 = |b|
MultBPos
        AND     R0,R0,#0
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     MultDone            ; |b|=0 -> product=0
MultLoop
        ADD     R0,R0,R1
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
        BRp     MultLoop
MultDone
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpMultR2
        ADD     R3,R3,#0
        BRz     MultPush
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
MultPush
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,OpMultR7
        BRnzp   NewCommand
OpMultR2
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpMultR1
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpMultExit
        LD      R7,OpMultR7
        BRnzp   NewCommand
OpMultR7  .FILL  x0000

; ============================================================
; OpMod  (a=top/dividend, b=second/divisor;  push a%b)
; Sign of result = sign of a.
; ============================================================
OpMod
        ST      R7,OpModR7
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpModExit
        ADD     R1,R0,#0            ; R1 = a
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpModR1
        ADD     R2,R0,#0            ; R2 = b
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     OpModZero
        AND     R3,R3,#0
        ADD     R4,R1,#0
        BRzp    ModAPos
        ADD     R3,R3,#1
        NOT     R4,R4
        ADD     R4,R4,#1            ; R4=|a|
ModAPos
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRzp    ModBPos
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1            ; R2=|b|
ModBPos
ModLoop
        NOT     R0,R2
        ADD     R0,R0,#1            ; R0 = -|b|
        ADD     R0,R4,R0
        BRn     ModDone
        ADD     R4,R0,#0
        BRnzp   ModLoop
ModDone
        ADD     R0,R4,#0
        ADD     R3,R3,#0
        BRz     ModSignOK
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
ModSignOK
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpModR2
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,OpModR7
        BRnzp   NewCommand
OpModZero
        LEA     R0,DivZeroMsg
        PUTS
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
        LD      R7,OpModR7
        BRnzp   NewCommand
OpModR2
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpModR1
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpModExit
        LD      R7,OpModR7
        BRnzp   NewCommand
OpModR7   .FILL  x0000
DivZeroMsg .FILL x000A
           .STRINGZ "Error: Division by zero."

; ============================================================
; OpXOR  (a=top, b=second;  push a XOR b)
; a XOR b = NOT( NOT(~a&b) AND NOT(a&~b) )
; ============================================================
OpXOR
        ST      R7,OpXORR7
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpXORExit
        ADD     R1,R0,#0
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpXORR1
        ADD     R2,R0,#0
        NOT     R3,R1
        AND     R3,R3,R2
        NOT     R4,R2
        AND     R4,R1,R4
        NOT     R3,R3
        NOT     R4,R4
        AND     R0,R3,R4
        NOT     R0,R0
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpXORR2
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,OpXORR7
        BRnzp   NewCommand
OpXORR2
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpXORR1
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpXORExit
        LD      R7,OpXORR7
        BRnzp   NewCommand
OpXORR7   .FILL  x0000

; ============================================================
; OpNeg  (negate top of stack)
; ============================================================
OpNeg
        ST      R7,OpNegR7
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpNegExit
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
        JSR     PUSH
OpNegExit
        LD      R7,OpNegR7
        BRnzp   NewCommand
OpNegR7   .FILL  x0000

; ============================================================
; OpDisplay  (show top of stack, no pop)
; ============================================================
OpDisplay
        ST      R7,OpDispR7
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpDispExit
        ST      R0,OpDispVal
        JSR     BinarytoASCII
        LD      R0,NewlineChar
        OUT
        LEA     R0,ASCIIBUFF
        PUTS
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1           ; undo the pop
OpDispExit
        LD      R7,OpDispR7
        BRnzp   NewCommand
OpDispR7  .FILL  x0000
OpDispVal .FILL  x0000
NewlineChar .FILL x000A

; ============================================================
; PushValue: R0 = first character (digit or '-'), already echoed
; Reads remaining digits until CR/LF, converts and pushes.
; ============================================================
PushValue
        ST      R7,PushValR7
        LEA     R1,ASCIIBUFF
        LD      R2,MaxDigits
        AND     R4,R4,#0            ; sign flag
        LD      R3,NegMinus2
        ADD     R3,R3,R0
        BRnp    PVNotMinus
        ADD     R4,R4,#1
        GETC
        OUT
PVNotMinus
        STR     R0,R1,#0
        ADD     R1,R1,#1
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
PVLoop
        GETC
        OUT
        ADD     R3,R0,#-13
        BRz     PVGood
        ADD     R3,R0,#-10
        BRz     PVGood
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     PVTooLarge
        STR     R0,R1,#0
        ADD     R1,R1,#1
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
        BRnzp   PVLoop
PVGood
        LEA     R2,ASCIIBUFF
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        ADD     R1,R1,R2            ; R1 = digit count
        JSR     ASCIItoBinary
        ADD     R4,R4,#0
        BRz     PVDoPush
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
PVDoPush
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,PushValR7
        BRnzp   NewCommand
PVTooLarge
PVFlush GETC
        OUT
        ADD     R3,R0,#-13
        BRz     PVFlushDone
        ADD     R3,R0,#-10
        BRnp    PVFlush
PVFlushDone
        LEA     R0,TooManyMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,PushValR7
        BRnzp   NewCommand
PushValR7 .FILL x0000
NegMinus2 .FILL xFFD3
MaxDigits .FILL #3
TooManyMsg .FILL x000A
           .STRINGZ "Too many digits"

; ============================================================
; PUSH subroutine
; In: R0 = value   Out: R5=0 ok | R5=1 full
; ============================================================
PUSH
        ST      R7,PUSHR7
        ST      R1,PUSHR1
        LEA     R1,StackMax
        NOT     R1,R1
        ADD     R1,R1,#1
        ADD     R1,R1,R6
        BRz     PUSHFull
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
        STR     R0,R6,#0
        LD      R1,PUSHR1
        LD      R7,PUSHR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        RET
PUSHFull
        ST      R7,PUSHR7B
        LEA     R0,OverflowMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,PUSHR7B
        LD      R1,PUSHR1
        LD      R7,PUSHR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1
        RET
PUSHR7   .FILL  x0000
PUSHR7B  .FILL  x0000
PUSHR1   .FILL  x0000
OverflowMsg .FILL x000A
            .STRINGZ "Error: Stack is Full."

; ============================================================
; POP subroutine
; Out: R0=value, R5=0 ok | R5=1 empty
; ============================================================
POP
        ST      R7,POPR7
        ST      R1,POPR1
        LEA     R1,StackBase
        NOT     R1,R1
        ADD     R1,R1,#1
        ADD     R1,R1,R6
        BRz     POPEmpty
        LDR     R0,R6,#0
        ADD     R6,R6,#1
        LD      R1,POPR1
        LD      R7,POPR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        RET
POPEmpty
        ST      R7,POPR7B
        LEA     R0,UnderflowMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,POPR7B
        LD      R1,POPR1
        LD      R7,POPR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1
        RET
POPR7    .FILL  x0000
POPR7B   .FILL  x0000
POPR1    .FILL  x0000
UnderflowMsg .FILL x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Too Few Values on the Stack."

; ============================================================
; RangeCheck subroutine
; In: R0 = value (preserved)   Out: R5=0 ok | R5=1 out-of-range
; ============================================================
RangeCheck
        ST      R7,RCR7
        ST      R0,RCR0
        LD      R4,Pos999
        ADD     R3,R0,R4            ; R0 + 999: negative if R0 < -999
        BRn     RCBad
        NOT     R3,R0
        ADD     R3,R3,#1            ; R3 = -R0
        ADD     R3,R4,R3            ; 999 + (-R0) = 999-R0: neg if R0>999
        BRn     RCBad
        LD      R0,RCR0
        LD      R7,RCR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        RET
RCBad
        ST      R7,RCR7B
        LEA     R0,RangeErrMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,RCR7B
        LD      R0,RCR0
        LD      R7,RCR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1
        RET
RCR7   .FILL  x0000
RCR7B  .FILL  x0000
RCR0   .FILL  x0000
Pos999 .FILL  #999
RangeErrMsg .FILL x000A
            .STRINGZ "Error: Number is out of range."

; ============================================================
; ASCIItoBinary
; In:  ASCIIBUFF[0..R1-1] = ASCII digits (most-significant first)
;      R1 = digit count (1..3)
; Out: R0 = binary value (unsigned; caller negates if needed)
; ============================================================
ASCIItoBinary
        AND     R0,R0,#0
        ADD     R1,R1,#0
        BRz     AtoBDone
        LD      R3,NegASCII
        LEA     R2,ASCIIBUFF
        ADD     R2,R2,R1
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3
        ADD     R0,R0,R4
        ADD     R1,R1,#-1
        BRz     AtoBDone
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3
        LEA     R5,LookUp10
        ADD     R5,R5,R4
        LDR     R4,R5,#0
        ADD     R0,R0,R4
        ADD     R1,R1,#-1
        BRz     AtoBDone
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3
        LEA     R5,LookUp100
        ADD     R5,R5,R4
        LDR     R4,R5,#0
        ADD     R0,R0,R4
AtoBDone
        RET
NegASCII .FILL xFFD0

; ============================================================
; BinarytoASCII
; In:  R0 = signed value [-999..999]
; Out: ASCIIBUFF = null-terminated string (sign + 3 digits)
; ============================================================
BinarytoASCII
        ST      R7,BtoAR7
        LEA     R1,ASCIIBUFF
        ADD     R0,R0,#0
        BRn     BtoANeg
        LD      R2,ASCIIPlus
        STR     R2,R1,#0
        BRnzp   BtoAH
BtoANeg
        LD      R2,ASCIIMinus
        STR     R2,R1,#0
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
BtoAH
        LD      R2,ASCIIOffset
        LD      R3,Neg100
BtoAH_Loop
        ADD     R0,R0,R3
        BRn     BtoAH_End
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        BRnzp   BtoAH_Loop
BtoAH_End
        STR     R2,R1,#1
        LD      R3,Pos100
        ADD     R0,R0,R3
        LD      R2,ASCIIOffset
        LD      R3,Neg10
BtoAT_Loop
        ADD     R0,R0,R3
        BRn     BtoAT_End
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        BRnzp   BtoAT_Loop
BtoAT_End
        STR     R2,R1,#2
        ADD     R0,R0,#10
        LD      R2,ASCIIOffset
        ADD     R2,R2,R0
        STR     R2,R1,#3
        AND     R2,R2,#0
        STR     R2,R1,#4
        LD      R7,BtoAR7
        RET
BtoAR7    .FILL  x0000
ASCIIPlus .FILL  x002B
ASCIIMinus .FILL x002D
ASCIIOffset .FILL x0030
Neg100    .FILL  xFF9C
Pos100    .FILL  x0064
Neg10     .FILL  xFFF6

; ============================================================
; DATA SECTION
; ============================================================
PromptMsg  .FILL  x000A
           .STRINGZ "Enter a command: "

ASCIIBUFF  .BLKW  6

LookUp10   .FILL  #0
           .FILL  #10
           .FILL  #20
           .FILL  #30
           .FILL  #40
           .FILL  #50
           .FILL  #60
           .FILL  #70
           .FILL  #80
           .FILL  #90

LookUp100  .FILL  #0
           .FILL  #100
           .FILL  #200
           .FILL  #300
           .FILL  #400
           .FILL  #500
           .FILL  #600
           .FILL  #700
           .FILL  #800
           .FILL  #900

StackMax   .BLKW  5
StackBase  .FILL  x0000

        .END
", "file_path": "/tmp/isolated_workspace_1091/lab2.asm"}

turns.content:
There are two main categories of problems:
1. Long-range `LEA StackBase/StackMax/PromptMsg/ASCIIBUFF` — data too far away
2. Long-range `BRnzp NewCommand` — the `NewCommand` label is too far back

The solution is to:
1. Move `StackBase`/`StackMax` and the stack to the **beginning** of the program (before x3000 code, or use a separate `.ORIG` for it at a high address)
2. Use a **jump table** or put `NewCommand` as a `JMP` target via a register
3. Move frequently-accessed data closer to the code

The cleanest approach for LC-3: put the stack at a **fixed high-memory address** using a separate `.ORIG`, and use a **trampoline** for `NewCommand` to avoid long backward branches. For `ASCIIBUFF` and `PromptMsg`, move them to be right below the first LD instruction.

Let me use **two `.ORIG` sections**: main code at x3000 and stack+buffer data at x3F00 (high memory). For the backward branch problem, I'll use `JMP R7`-style or store the `NewCommand` address in memory for indirect jump. Actually, the most common LC-3 approach is to use an indirect jump: `LD R0, NewCmdPtr; JMP R0`.

Actually wait — a simpler fix: use `JMP Rx` with a stored address. But most LC-3 programs that are long use `.ORIG` to place data at a known location. Let me restructure more carefully:

tool_calls.name: write
tool_calls.arguments:
{"content": "; ============================================================
; LC-3 Stack-Based Calculator
;
; Stack at x3F00 area (see second .ORIG section at end)
; Main code at x3000
;
; Stack conventions:
;   R6 = stack pointer
;   Empty: R6 == addr(StackBase)   Full: R6 == addr(StackMax)
;   PUSH: check full; R6--; M[R6]=R0
;   POP : check empty; R0=M[R6]; R6++
;   R5=0 success, R5=1 failure
;   Results in [-999,999]; else error + stack restored
; ============================================================

; ============================================================
; CONSTANTS / DATA SECTION (placed first for PC-offset reach)
; ============================================================
        .ORIG   x3000

; Prompt (kept near NewCommand which uses LEA)
PromptMsg   .FILL   x000A
            .STRINGZ "Enter a command: "

; Dispatch character negatives (near Test)
NegX        .FILL   xFFA8
NegC        .FILL   xFFBD
NegPlus     .FILL   xFFD5
NegMult     .FILL   xFFD6
NegPct      .FILL   xFFDB
NegAt       .FILL   xFFC0
NegN        .FILL   xFF92
NegD        .FILL   xFFBC
NegMinus    .FILL   xFFD3
NegZero     .FILL   xFFD0
NegNine     .FILL   xFFC7
NegE        .FILL   xFF9B
NegG        .FILL   xFF99

; ASCII buffer for number I/O
ASCIIBUFF   .BLKW   6

; Digit conversion tables
LookUp10    .FILL   #0
            .FILL   #10
            .FILL   #20
            .FILL   #30
            .FILL   #40
            .FILL   #50
            .FILL   #60
            .FILL   #70
            .FILL   #80
            .FILL   #90

LookUp100   .FILL   #0
            .FILL   #100
            .FILL   #200
            .FILL   #300
            .FILL   #400
            .FILL   #500
            .FILL   #600
            .FILL   #700
            .FILL   #800
            .FILL   #900

; Numeric constants
Pos999      .FILL   #999
NegASCII    .FILL   xFFD0
ASCIIPlus   .FILL   x002B
ASCIIMinus  .FILL   x002D
ASCIIOffset .FILL   x0030
Neg100      .FILL   xFF9C
Pos100      .FILL   x0064
Neg10       .FILL   xFFF6
MaxDigits   .FILL   #3

; Miscellaneous constants
NewlineChar .FILL   x000A
NegMinus2   .FILL   xFFD3       ; second copy for PushValue

; Pointer to NewCommand (used for long-range JMP)
NCPtr       .FILL   x3068       ; will be patched: see note below

; ============================================================
; CODE SECTION
; ============================================================
        .ORIG   x3060

Start
        ; Initialize stack pointer to StackBase (empty stack)
        LD      R0,SBase
        ADD     R6,R0,#0        ; R6 = StackBase address

; NewCommand: prompt and read a character
NewCommand
        LEA     R0,PromptMsg
        PUTS
        GETC
        OUT

; Test: dispatch on character in R0
Test
        LD      R1,NegX
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     ExitProg

        LD      R1,NegC
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpClear

        LD      R1,NegPlus
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpAdd

        LD      R1,NegMult
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpMult

        LD      R1,NegPct
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpMod

        LD      R1,NegAt
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpXOR

        LD      R1,NegN
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoReadNeg

        LD      R1,NegD
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoOpDisplay

        LD      R1,NegMinus
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     DoPushValue

        LD      R1,NegZero
        ADD     R2,R0,R1
        BRn     NewCommand
        LD      R1,NegNine
        ADD     R2,R0,R1
        BRp     NewCommand
        BRnzp   DoPushValue

; ---- Trampoline stubs (keep close to Test for 9-bit BR offsets) ----
DoOpClear
        JSR     OpClear
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpAdd
        JSR     OpAdd
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpMult
        JSR     OpMult
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpMod
        JSR     OpMod
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpXOR
        JSR     OpXOR
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoReadNeg
        JSR     ReadNeg
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoOpDisplay
        JSR     OpDisplay
        BRnzp   NewCommand
DoPushValue
        JSR     PushValue
        BRnzp   NewCommand

ExitProg
        HALT

; Stack base/max pointers for Init
SBase       .FILL   x3FF8       ; addr of StackBase (see data .ORIG x3FF0 below)
SMax        .FILL   x3FF3       ; addr of StackMax  (StackBase - 5)

; ============================================================
; ReadNeg: consume 'e','g' after 'n' to confirm "neg"
; (R0 = 'n' on entry, already echoed)
; ============================================================
ReadNeg
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R1,NegE
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRnp    ReadNegDone     ; not 'e', just return
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R1,NegG
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRnp    ReadNegDone     ; not 'g', just return
        JSR     OpNeg
ReadNegDone
        RET

; ============================================================
; OpClear
; ============================================================
OpClear
        LD      R6,SBase
        RET

; ============================================================
; OpAdd: pop a(top), pop b; push b+a
; ============================================================
OpAdd
        ST      R7,OpAddR7
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpAddEx
        ADD     R1,R0,#0
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpAddR1
        ADD     R0,R0,R1
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpAddR2
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,OpAddR7
        RET
OpAddR2
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpAddR1
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpAddEx
        LD      R7,OpAddR7
        RET
OpAddR7 .FILL   x0000

; ============================================================
; OpMult: pop a(top), pop b; push b*a
; ============================================================
OpMult
        ST      R7,OpMultR7
        AND     R3,R3,#0
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpMultEx
        ADD     R1,R0,#0
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpMultR1
        ADD     R1,R1,#0
        BRzp    MultAPos
        NOT     R3,R3
        AND     R3,R3,#1
        NOT     R1,R1
        ADD     R1,R1,#1
MultAPos
        ADD     R2,R0,#0
        BRzp    MultBPos
        NOT     R3,R3
        AND     R3,R3,#1
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
MultBPos
        AND     R0,R0,#0
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     MultDone
MultLoop
        ADD     R0,R0,R1
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
        BRp     MultLoop
MultDone

        ADD     R5,R5,#0

        ADD     R3,R3,#0

MultPush

OpMultR2

OpMultR1

OpMultEx

        ADD     R3,R3,#1

ModAPos
        ADD     R2,R2,#0

        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
ModBPos
ModLoop

ModSignOK

        LD      R7,OpModR7

OpModR2
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpModR1
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpModEx

OpXORR1

OpXORExit

OpDispVal .FILL  x0000

RCR7   .FILL  x0000
RCR7B  .FILL  x0000
RCR0   .FILL  x0000
RangeErrMsg .FILL x000A

; ============================================================

ASCIItoBinary

turns.content:
The problem is that by putting constants at the beginning (x3000) and code at x3060, the subroutines at the end (x3200+) are too far from those constants. I need to move the constants closer to each subroutine that uses them — i.e., place them right after each subroutine's RET (as the reference snippets do).

Let me restructure: **all subroutine-specific constants go right after that subroutine**, and shared constants (like ASCIIBUFF, lookup tables) go close to the subroutines that access them most (ASCIItoBinary and BinarytoASCII):

tool_calls.name: write
tool_calls.arguments:
{"content": "; ============================================================
; LC-3 Stack-Based Calculator
;
; Stack: 5 cells at x3FF3-x3FF7, StackBase sentinel at x3FF8
; R6 = stack pointer; empty: R6==x3FF8; full: R6==x3FF3
; R5=0 success  R5=1 failure
; Result range [-999,999]; else error + stack restored.
;
; Memory layout:
;   x3000 - Main loop + dispatch constants
;   x3060 - Subroutines
;   x3FF3 - Stack (5 cells) + StackBase sentinel
; ============================================================

; ============================================================
; MAIN LOOP  (with inline data close to each LD instruction)
; ============================================================
        .ORIG   x3000

        ; Initialize stack pointer
        LD      R6,InitSBase        ; R6 = x3FF8 (empty)

NewCommand
        LEA     R0,PromptMsg
        PUTS
        GETC
        OUT

; -- Dispatch --
Test
        LD      R1,NegX
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     ExitProg

        LD      R1,NegC
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     GoOpClear

        LD      R1,NegPlus
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     GoOpAdd

        LD      R1,NegMult
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     GoOpMult

        LD      R1,NegPct
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     GoOpMod

        LD      R1,NegAt
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     GoOpXOR

        LD      R1,NegN
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     GoReadNeg

        LD      R1,NegD
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     GoOpDisplay

        LD      R1,NegMinus
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     GoValue

        LD      R1,NegZero
        ADD     R2,R0,R1
        BRn     NewCommand
        LD      R1,NegNine
        ADD     R2,R0,R1
        BRp     NewCommand
        BRnzp   GoValue

; -- Data for dispatch (kept within ±255 of Test) --
InitSBase   .FILL   x3FF8           ; addr(StackBase)
PromptMsg   .FILL   x000A
            .STRINGZ "Enter a command: "
NegX        .FILL   xFFA8
NegC        .FILL   xFFBD
NegPlus     .FILL   xFFD5
NegMult     .FILL   xFFD6
NegPct      .FILL   xFFDB
NegAt       .FILL   xFFC0
NegN        .FILL   xFF92
NegD        .FILL   xFFBC
NegMinus    .FILL   xFFD3
NegZero     .FILL   xFFD0
NegNine     .FILL   xFFC7
NegE        .FILL   xFF9B
NegG        .FILL   xFF99

; -- Trampolines to subroutines + return to NewCommand --
GoOpClear
        JSR     OpClear
        BRnzp   NewCommand
GoOpAdd
        JSR     OpAdd
        BRnzp   NewCommand
GoOpMult
        JSR     OpMult
        BRnzp   NewCommand
GoOpMod
        JSR     OpMod
        BRnzp   NewCommand
GoOpXOR
        JSR     OpXOR
        BRnzp   NewCommand
GoReadNeg
        JSR     ReadNeg
        BRnzp   NewCommand
GoOpDisplay
        JSR     OpDisplay
        BRnzp   NewCommand
GoValue
        JSR     PushValue
        BRnzp   NewCommand

ExitProg
        HALT

; ============================================================
; ReadNeg: read 'e','g' after 'n' to confirm "neg"
; ============================================================
ReadNeg
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R1,NegE
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRnp    ReadNegRet
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R1,NegG
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRnp    ReadNegRet
        JSR     OpNeg
ReadNegRet
        RET

; ============================================================
; OpClear
; ============================================================
OpClear
        LD      R6,InitSBase
        RET

; ============================================================
; OpAdd: pop a(top), pop b; push b+a
; ============================================================
OpAdd
        ST      R7,OpAddR7
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpAddEx
        ADD     R1,R0,#0
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpAddR1
        ADD     R0,R0,R1
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpAddR2
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,OpAddR7
        RET
OpAddR2 ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpAddR1 ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpAddEx LD      R7,OpAddR7
        RET
OpAddR7 .FILL   x0000

; ============================================================
; OpMult: pop a(top), pop b; push b*a
; ============================================================
OpMult
        ST      R7,OpMultR7
        AND     R3,R3,#0
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpMultEx
        ADD     R1,R0,#0
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpMultR1
        ; sign of a
        ADD     R1,R1,#0
        BRzp    MultAPos
        NOT     R3,R3
        AND     R3,R3,#1
        NOT     R1,R1
        ADD     R1,R1,#1        ; R1 = |a|
MultAPos
        ; sign of b (in R0)
        ADD     R2,R0,#0
        BRzp    MultBPos
        NOT     R3,R3
        AND     R3,R3,#1
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1        ; R2 = |b|
MultBPos
        AND     R0,R0,#0
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     MultDone
MultLoop
        ADD     R0,R0,R1
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
        BRp     MultLoop
MultDone
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpMultR2
        ADD     R3,R3,#0
        BRz     MultPush
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
MultPush
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,OpMultR7
        RET
OpMultR2 ADD    R6,R6,#-1
OpMultR1 ADD    R6,R6,#-1
OpMultEx LD     R7,OpMultR7
        RET
OpMultR7 .FILL  x0000

; ============================================================
; OpMod: pop a(top/dividend), pop b(divisor); push a%b
; Sign of result = sign of a
; ============================================================
OpMod
        ST      R7,OpModR7
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpModEx
        ADD     R1,R0,#0        ; R1 = a
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpModR1
        ADD     R2,R0,#0        ; R2 = b
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     OpModZero
        AND     R3,R3,#0
        ADD     R4,R1,#0
        BRzp    ModAPos
        ADD     R3,R3,#1
        NOT     R4,R4
        ADD     R4,R4,#1        ; R4 = |a|
ModAPos
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRzp    ModBPos
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1        ; R2 = |b|
ModBPos
ModLoop NOT     R0,R2
        ADD     R0,R0,#1        ; R0 = -|b|
        ADD     R0,R4,R0        ; R0 = running - |b|
        BRn     ModDone
        ADD     R4,R0,#0
        BRnzp   ModLoop
ModDone
        ADD     R0,R4,#0
        ADD     R3,R3,#0
        BRz     ModSignOK
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
ModSignOK
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpModR2
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,OpModR7
        RET
OpModZero
        LEA     R0,DivZeroMsg
        PUTS
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
        LD      R7,OpModR7
        RET
OpModR2 ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpModR1 ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpModEx LD      R7,OpModR7
        RET
OpModR7    .FILL  x0000
DivZeroMsg .FILL  x000A
           .STRINGZ "Error: Division by zero."

; ============================================================
; OpXOR: pop a(top), pop b; push a XOR b
; ============================================================
OpXOR
        ST      R7,OpXORR7
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpXORExit
        ADD     R1,R0,#0
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpXORR1
        ADD     R2,R0,#0
        NOT     R3,R1
        AND     R3,R3,R2
        NOT     R4,R2
        AND     R4,R1,R4
        NOT     R3,R3
        NOT     R4,R4
        AND     R0,R3,R4
        NOT     R0,R0
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpXORR2
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,OpXORR7
        RET
OpXORR2  ADD    R6,R6,#-1
OpXORR1  ADD    R6,R6,#-1
OpXORExit LD    R7,OpXORR7
        RET
OpXORR7  .FILL  x0000

; ============================================================
; OpNeg: negate top of stack
; ============================================================
OpNeg
        ST      R7,OpNegR7
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpNegExit
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
        JSR     PUSH
OpNegExit
        LD      R7,OpNegR7
        RET
OpNegR7  .FILL  x0000

; ============================================================
; OpDisplay: show top of stack without popping
; ============================================================
OpDisplay
        ST      R7,OpDispR7
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpDispExit
        ST      R0,OpDispVal
        JSR     BinarytoASCII
        LD      R0,NewlineChar
        OUT
        LEA     R0,ASCIIBUFF
        PUTS
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1       ; undo pop
OpDispExit
        LD      R7,OpDispR7
        RET
OpDispR7   .FILL  x0000
OpDispVal  .FILL  x0000
NewlineChar .FILL x000A

; ============================================================
; PushValue: R0 = first char (echoed), digit or '-'
; ============================================================
PushValue
        ST      R7,PushVR7
        LEA     R1,ASCIIBUFF
        LD      R2,MaxDigits
        AND     R4,R4,#0
        LD      R3,NegMinus2
        ADD     R3,R3,R0
        BRnp    PVNotMinus
        ADD     R4,R4,#1
        GETC
        OUT
PVNotMinus
        STR     R0,R1,#0
        ADD     R1,R1,#1
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
PVLoop
        GETC
        OUT
        ADD     R3,R0,#-13      ; CR?
        BRz     PVGood
        ADD     R3,R0,#-10      ; LF?
        BRz     PVGood
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     PVTooLarge
        STR     R0,R1,#0
        ADD     R1,R1,#1
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
        BRnzp   PVLoop
PVGood
        LEA     R2,ASCIIBUFF
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        ADD     R1,R1,R2        ; R1 = digit count
        JSR     ASCIItoBinary
        ADD     R4,R4,#0
        BRz     PVDoPush
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
PVDoPush
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,PushVR7
        RET
PVTooLarge
PVFlush GETC
        OUT
        ADD     R3,R0,#-13
        BRz     PVFlushDone
        ADD     R3,R0,#-10
        BRnp    PVFlush
PVFlushDone
        LEA     R0,TooManyMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,PushVR7
        RET
PushVR7   .FILL  x0000
NegMinus2 .FILL  xFFD3
MaxDigits .FILL  #3
TooManyMsg .FILL x000A
           .STRINGZ "Too many digits"

; ============================================================
; PUSH subroutine
; ============================================================
PUSH
        ST      R7,PUSHR7
        ST      R1,PUSHR1
        LD      R1,PushSMax     ; R1 = addr(StackMax)
        NOT     R1,R1
        ADD     R1,R1,#1
        ADD     R1,R1,R6
        BRz     PUSHFull
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
        STR     R0,R6,#0
        LD      R1,PUSHR1
        LD      R7,PUSHR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        RET
PUSHFull
        ST      R7,PUSHR7B
        LEA     R0,OverflowMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,PUSHR7B
        LD      R1,PUSHR1
        LD      R7,PUSHR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1
        RET
PUSHR7      .FILL  x0000
PUSHR7B     .FILL  x0000
PUSHR1      .FILL  x0000
PushSMax    .FILL  x3FF3        ; addr(StackMax)
OverflowMsg .FILL  x000A
            .STRINGZ "Error: Stack is Full."

; ============================================================
; POP subroutine
; ============================================================
POP
        ST      R7,POPR7
        ST      R1,POPR1
        LD      R1,PopSBase     ; R1 = addr(StackBase)
        NOT     R1,R1
        ADD     R1,R1,#1
        ADD     R1,R1,R6
        BRz     POPEmpty
        LDR     R0,R6,#0
        ADD     R6,R6,#1
        LD      R1,POPR1
        LD      R7,POPR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        RET
POPEmpty
        ST      R7,POPR7B
        LEA     R0,UnderflowMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,POPR7B
        LD      R1,POPR1
        LD      R7,POPR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1
        RET
POPR7        .FILL  x0000
POPR7B       .FILL  x0000
POPR1        .FILL  x0000
PopSBase     .FILL  x3FF8       ; addr(StackBase)
UnderflowMsg .FILL  x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Too Few Values on the Stack."

; ============================================================
; RangeCheck subroutine
; ============================================================
RangeCheck
        ST      R7,RCR7
        ST      R0,RCR0
        LD      R4,RCPos999
        ADD     R3,R0,R4        ; R0 + 999: neg if R0 < -999
        BRn     RCBad
        NOT     R3,R0
        ADD     R3,R3,#1        ; -R0
        ADD     R3,R4,R3        ; 999-R0: neg if R0 > 999
        BRn     RCBad
        LD      R0,RCR0
        LD      R7,RCR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        RET
RCBad
        ST      R7,RCR7B
        LEA     R0,RangeErrMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,RCR7B
        LD      R0,RCR0
        LD      R7,RCR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1
        RET
RCR7        .FILL  x0000
RCR7B       .FILL  x0000
RCR0        .FILL  x0000
RCPos999    .FILL  #999
RangeErrMsg .FILL  x000A
            .STRINGZ "Error: Number is out of range."

; ============================================================
; ASCIItoBinary
; In:  ASCIIBUFF[0..R1-1] = digits (MSB first)
;      R1 = digit count
; Out: R0 = binary value
; ============================================================
ASCIItoBinary
        AND     R0,R0,#0
        ADD     R1,R1,#0
        BRz     AtoBDone
        LD      R3,NegASCII

        ADD     R2,R2,R1
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1           ; -> ones digit
        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3
        ADD     R0,R0,R4
        ADD     R1,R1,#-1
        BRz     AtoBDone
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1           ; -> tens digit
        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3
        LEA     R5,LookUp10
        ADD     R5,R5,R4
        LDR     R4,R5,#0
        ADD     R0,R0,R4
        ADD     R1,R1,#-1
        BRz     AtoBDone
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1           ; -> hundreds digit
        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3
        LEA     R5,LookUp100
        ADD     R5,R5,R4
        LDR     R4,R5,#0
        ADD     R0,R0,R4
AtoBDone
        RET
NegASCII .FILL  xFFD0

; ============================================================
; BinarytoASCII
; In:  R0 = signed value [-999..999]
; Out: ASCIIBUFF = null-terminated (sign + 3 digits)
; ============================================================
BinarytoASCII

        ADD     R0,R0,#0
        BRn     BtoANeg
        LD      R2,ASCIIPlus
        STR     R2,R1,#0
        BRnzp   BtoAH
BtoANeg
        LD      R2,ASCIIMinus
        STR     R2,R1,#0
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
BtoAH
        LD      R2,ASCIIOffset
        LD      R3,Neg100
BtoAHL
        ADD     R0,R0,R3
        BRn     BtoAHE
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        BRnzp   BtoAHL
BtoAHE
        STR     R2,R1,#1
        LD      R3,Pos100
        ADD     R0,R0,R3
        LD      R2,ASCIIOffset
        LD      R3,Neg10
BtoATL
        ADD     R0,R0,R3
        BRn     BtoATE
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        BRnzp   BtoATL
BtoATE
        STR     R2,R1,#2
        ADD     R0,R0,#10
        LD      R2,ASCIIOffset
        ADD     R2,R2,R0
        STR     R2,R1,#3
        AND     R2,R2,#0
        STR     R2,R1,#4
        LD      R7,BtoAR7
        RET

ASCIIMinus .FILL  x002D

turns.content:
Getting closer — only 3 errors now, all `LEA ASCIIBUFF` from `OpDisplay` and `PushValue`. The issue is that `ASCIIBUFF` is at x3291 but `OpDisplay` is at x3134 (offset 339) and `PushValue` is at x3148 (offset 328). I need to move `ASCIIBUFF` closer to where those routines are.

The fix: move `ASCIIBUFF` right before `BinarytoASCII` is fine for `BinarytoASCII` but not for the display/push routines. I'll place `ASCIIBUFF` between `OpDisplay`/`PushValue` and the subroutines below them. Specifically, right after `OpNeg` (around x3135) and within 255 of `ASCIItoBinary` (x3240) and `BinarytoASCII` (x3260).

x3135 + 255 = x3234, so if ASCIIBUFF is at ~x3190 (between OpNeg and PUSH), all LEA ASCIIBUFF from x3134, x3148, x3163 would have offsets of ~+60 to +90 (fine), and from ASCIItoBinary at x3240 the offset would be ~-80 (fine). Let me move ASCIIBUFF there:

turns.content:
The file is complete. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

---

## `lab2.asm` — Complete LC-3 Stack Calculator

**654 lines** of well-structured LC-3 assembly. Key design decisions:

### Architecture
- **Two `.ORIG` segments**: main code at `x3000`, stack at `x3FF3`
- **5-slot stack**: `StackMax`=`x3FF3`…`StackBase`=`x3FF8` (sentinel)
- **Trampoline pattern**: main dispatch calls `JSR OpXxx` then `BRnzp NewCommand` — keeps all backward branches within ±255 words

### Memory Layout (solved PC-offset problem)
- Dispatch constants (`NegX`, `NegC`, …) placed right after `Test` code → within ±255 of all dispatch `LD`s
- Subroutine-local data (save slots, error strings) placed right after each subroutine's `RET`
- `ASCIIBUFF` + lookup tables placed between `PushValue`/`OpDisplay` and the `ASCIItoBinary`/`BinarytoASCII` subroutines, ensuring all `LEA ASCIIBUFF` are in range

### Operations Implemented
| Command | Logic |
|---------|-------|
| `X` | `HALT` |
| `C` | Reset R6 to `StackBase` |
| `+` | Pop a, pop b → push b+a; range-check; restore on fail |
| `*` | Pop a, pop b → push b×a; sign-tracking via XOR flag; restore on fail |
| `%` | Pop a (dividend), pop b (divisor) → push a%b; handles sign; div-by-zero guard |
| `@` | Pop a, pop b → push a XOR b using `NOT`/`AND` |
| `neg` | Reads `e`,`g` chars; pop → negate → push |
| `D` | Pop → display via `BinarytoASCII` → undo pop (SP-1) |
| digits | Read until CR/LF, convert via `ASCIItoBinary`, push; supports leading `-` |

### Error Handling
- Stack overflow/underflow: prints error, sets R5=1
- Out of range [−999,999]: prints error, restores both stack slots (ADD R6,R6,#-1 twice)
- Division by zero: prints error, restores both stack slots
- Too many digits (>3): flushes line, prints warning

deductions: Command dispatch incomplete, only detected 5/8 commands
Missing stack restore (Restore) logic for operation failures

IV. Code Quality LLM Judge (40 pts).4.1 Logical correctness (15): 10/15 - Main loop dispatch mostly correct for single-char ops (X,C,+,*,%,@,D) and routes 'n' into a follow-up reader for "neg". PUSH/POP implement a downward-growing stack with empty at x3FF8 and full at x3FF3, and set R5=0/1 as required. Binary ops do POP/POP/compute/PUSH in the right operand order. However, stack restoration on error is incorrect: in OpAdd/OpMult/OpMod/OpXOR, the code attempts to "undo" pops with `ADD R6,R6,#-1` (which actually moves SP further down, not back up), and it never writes the popped values back, so the stack becomes corrupted on underflow or range errors. OpNeg does not range-check the negated result (negating -999 is fine, but general requirement says range check results). OpDisplay pops then reverts with `ADD R6,R6,#-1`, which is also the wrong direction and does not restore the value; display will corrupt the stack.
IV. Code Quality LLM Judge (40 pts).4.2 Assembleability (10): 6/10 - Up to the truncation point, directives and instruction syntax look valid. Label usage for dispatch constants and subroutines is consistent. However, the submission is explicitly truncated at `ASCIItoBinary`/`BinarytoASCII` with `[...] truncated for grading ...]`, so as provided it will not assemble (missing code/labels, likely missing `.END`). Also, several comments claim LEA/LD are within ±255; without full layout it cannot be guaranteed, but the code shows attempts to keep data close.
IV. Code Quality LLM Judge (40 pts).4.3 Edge case handling (10): 3/10 - RangeCheck logic for [-999,999] is essentially correct and reports an error, setting R5=1. Stack overflow/underflow are detected in PUSH/POP and print messages. But the required behavior "restore stack on error" is not met (incorrect SP adjustments and no value restoration). Modulo handles divisor=0 by printing an error, but again tries to restore by moving SP the wrong way. Input number parsing limits to 3 digits, but handling of negative values is inconsistent: PushValue stores '-' into ASCIIBUFF and then also separately negates the parsed result, which will only work if ASCIItoBinary ignores the '-' (unknown due to truncation). BinarytoASCII/ASCIItoBinary correctness cannot be verified due to truncation.
IV. Code Quality LLM Judge (40 pts).4.4 Completeness (5): 3/5 - Implements exit (X), clear (C), add (+), multiply (*), modulo (%), XOR (@), negate (via "neg"), display (D), and number entry (PushValue). However, the conversion subroutines (ASCIItoBinary/BinarytoASCII) are not actually present in the submitted text (truncated), so the program is not complete/standalone as required.
IV. Code Quality LLM Judge (40 pts).LLM summary: Good overall structure (dispatch + stack abstractions) and correct basic POP/compute/PUSH flow, but major correctness issues: stack restoration is wrong throughout (SP moved the wrong way and values not restored), and display corrupts the stack. As submitted it also will not assemble due to truncate

tool_results.content:
# Query: LC-3 Stack-Based Calculator

## Task Description

Implement a stack-based calculator program using **LC-3 assembly**. The program reads commands and operands from user input and supports the following features:

- **X**: Exit.
- **C**: Clear the stack.
- **+**, **\***, **%**, **@** (XOR), **neg** (negate): Pop operand(s) from the stack top, perform the operation, and push the result; the result must be within [-999, 999], otherwise report an error and restore the stack.
- **D**: Display the stack top (without popping).
- **Digits**: Push a valid integer onto the stack.

The stack grows from high address to low address. The stack region can be a fixed range (e.g., x3FFF to x3FFB). **R6** is the stack pointer. You must implement **PUSH** and **POP** subroutines, with the convention that **R5=0** indicates success and **R5=1** indicates failure (stack empty/full, etc.).

## Workflow

1. **Read context**
   - Read `context/problem_context.md` for LC-3 conventions, stack, and instruction set details.
   - Read `context/operation_list.md` for recommended implementation steps and correspondence with reference code in context.

2. **Implement PUSH / POP**
   - Refer to the stack implementations in context (e.g., 10.push.asm, 10.pop.asm), implement PUSH and POP that follow the task conventions, and correctly maintain R6 and R5.

3. **Implement operations and main loop**
   - Implement OpAdd, OpMult, Opmod, OpXOR, Opneg, OpClear, OpDisplay, etc. The main loop dispatches to the corresponding handler based on the input character, or pushes a number.
   - Pay attention to range checking (e.g., -999 to 999) and restoring the stack on error.

4. **Output**
   - Save the complete LC-3 assembly program to **lab2.asm** (or **answer.asm** / **solution.asm**) in the current directory. The program should begin with `.ORIG x3000` and include all subroutines and the data section.

## Constraints and Assumptions (from Context)

- Use the LC-3 instruction set; stack region and register conventions are specified in context.
- Do not depend on external tools or files other than those in context.
- The .asm files in context are reference snippets (corresponding to textbook figures); you may reference their logic and interfaces, but must independently produce a complete, assembleable, runnable program.

## Deliverables

- **lab2.asm** (or answer.asm / solution.asm): Complete LC-3 assembly source program, including main program, PUSH/POP, all operation subroutines, and data definitions.

## Notes

- Ensure POP returns failure when the stack is empty and PUSH returns failure when the stack is full; when operation results are out of range, report an error and restore the stack as required.
- Evaluation is performed by eval_task calling eval/rubric.py; context only contains background material and reference code, not the evaluation script.

# Operation List: LC-3 Stack Calculator Implementation Steps

## 1. Read the Problem and Context

- Read `workspace/query.md` for command descriptions and deliverable requirements.
- Read `context/problem_context.md` for stack conventions, R5/R6 interface, and range requirements.
- Browse the .asm reference snippets under context (e.g., 10.push.asm, 10.pop.asm, 10.10.asm through 10.27.asm), corresponding to the main loop, OpAdd, OpMult, POP, PUSH, and other logic.

## 2. Implement Stack Subroutines

- **PUSH**: Check if the stack is full (compare R6 with MAX). If full, set R5=1 and return; otherwise decrement R6 by 1, store R0 into (R6), set R5=0, and return.
- **POP**: Check if the stack is empty (compare R6 with BASE). If empty, set R5=1 and return; otherwise load from (R6) into R0, increment R6 by 1, set R5=0, and return.
- Refer to implementations in `10.push.asm`, `10.pop.asm`, or `10.23.asm`, etc.

## 3. Implement Main Loop and Command Dispatch

- Initialization: Set R6 to the stack base (e.g., StackBase), display a prompt "Enter a command:", and use GETC to read a character.
- Branch based on the character: X -> Exit, C -> OpClear, + -> OpAdd, * -> OpMult, % -> Opmod, @ -> OpXOR, neg -> Opneg, D -> OpDisplay, digit -> PushValue.
- Refer to the main program structure in context (e.g., the Test/NewCommand portion of reference_lab2.asm).

## 4. Implement Operations and Helper Routines

- **OpAdd / OpMult / Opmod / OpXOR**: Two POPs, perform the operation, RangeCheck; if successful, PUSH the result and continue; if failed, restore the stack (adjust R6) and return.
- **Opneg**: One POP, negate, RangeCheck, PUSH.
- **RangeCheck**: If the result is outside [-999, 999], print an error and return R5=1.
- **OpClear**: Point R6 to the stack base. **OpDisplay**: Display the stack top (without POP).
- Refer to snippets 10.10, 10.14, 10.15, 10.26, etc. in context.

## 5. Output and Deliverables

- Save the complete program as **lab2.asm** (or answer.asm / solution.asm) in the current directory.
- The program should include .ORIG x3000, all subroutines, and the data section (stack, prompt strings, constants, etc.).
- Evaluation is performed by eval_task calling eval/rubric.py; context only contains background material and reference code, not the evaluation script.

# Problem Context: LC-3 Stack Calculator

## LC-3 Conventions

- **Stack**: Grows from high address to low address; common stack region BASE is x3FFF, MAX is x3FFB; **R6** is the stack pointer (points to the top element).
- **PUSH**: Decrement R6 by 1 then write R0; if R6 has reached MAX, the stack is full and R5=1 indicates failure; otherwise R5=0.
- **POP**: Read from R6 into R0, then increment R6 by 1; if the stack is empty, R5=1; otherwise R5=0.
- **Operations**: Results must be within **[-999, 999]**; otherwise report an error and restore the stack (do not modify stack contents).

## Instructions and Commands

- Command **X**: Exit (HALT).
- **C**: Clear the stack (reinitialize R6).
- **+**, **\***, **%**, **@** (XOR), **neg**: Binary or unary operations; POP operands, compute, RangeCheck, PUSH result.
- **D**: Display the stack top (without popping).
- Digits: Push an integer onto the stack (must handle multi-digit input).

## Context File Descriptions

- `10.push.asm`, `10.pop.asm`, etc. are reference snippets (corresponding to textbook figures), containing PUSH/POP and some Op implementation ideas.
- The various .asm snippets can be referenced for interfaces and flow; your solution must be completed independently.
- All .asm files are LC-3 assembly, using `.ORIG`, `.FILL`, `.STRINGZ`, `.BLKW` and other pseudo-instructions.

## Constraints

- Do not rely on special extensions of external assemblers or simulators; use only standard LC-3 instructions and pseudo-instructions.
- Stack operation and computation interface conventions (R5 success/failure, R6 stack pointer) must match the task requirements.

 OpAdd       JSR     POP             ; Get first source operand.
             ADD     R5,R5,#0        ; Test if POP was successful.
             BRp     Exit            ; Branch if not successful.
             ADD     R1,R0,#0        ; Make room for second operand
             JSR     POP             ; Get second source operand.
             ADD     R5,R5,#0        ; Test if POP was successful.
             BRp     Restore1        ; Not successful, put back first.
             ADD     R0,R0,R1        ; THE Add.
             JSR     RangeCheck      ; Check size of result.
             BRp     Restore2        ; Out of range, restore both.
             JSR     PUSH            ; Push sum on the stack.
             RET                     ; On to the next task...
Restore2     ADD     R6,R6,#-1       ; Decrement stack pointer.
Restore1     ADD     R6,R6,#-1       ; Decrement stack pointer.
Exit         RET

;
;     Routine to check that the magnitude of a value is 
;     between -999 and +999.
;
RangeCheck    LD        R5,Neg999
              ADD       R4,R0,R5   ; Recall that R0 contains the 
              BRp       BadRange   ; result being checked.
              LD        R5,Pos999
              ADD       R4,R0,R5
              BRn       BadRange
              AND       R5,R5,#0   ; R5 <-- success
              RET
BadRange      ST        R7,Save    ; R7 is needed by TRAP/RET
              LEA       R0,RangeErrorMsg
              TRAP      x22        ; Output character string
              LD        R7,Save
              AND       R5,R5,#0   ; 
              ADD       R5,R5,#1   ; R5 <-- failure
              RET
Neg999        .FILL     #-999
Pos999        .FILL     #999
Save          .FILL     x0000
RangeErrorMsg .FILL     x000A
              .STRINGZ  "Error: Number is out of range."

;   
;    Algorithm to pop two values from the stack, multiply them
;    and if their product is within the acceptable range, push
;    the result on the stack.  R6 is stack pointer.
;    
OpMult         AND    R3,R3,#0      ; R3 holds sign of multiplier.
               JSR    POP           ; Get first source from stack.
               ADD    R5,R5,#0      ; Test for successful POP
               BRp    Exit          ; Failure
               ADD    R1,R0,#0      ; Make room for next POP
               JSR    POP           ; Get second source operand
               ADD    R5,R5,#0      ; Test for successful POP
               BRp    Restore1      ; Failure; restore first POP
               ADD    R2,R0,#0      ; Moves multiplier, tests sign
               BRzp   PosMultiplier 
               ADD    R3,R3,#1      ; Sets FLAG: Multiplier is neg
               NOT    R2,R2
               ADD    R2,R2,#1      ; R2 contains -(multiplier)
PosMultiplier  AND    R0,R0,#0      ; Clear product register
               ADD    R2,R2,#0
               BRz    PushMult      ; Multiplier = 0, Done.
;
MultLoop       ADD    R0,R0,R1      ; THE actual "multiply"
               ADD    R2,R2,#-1     ; Iteration Control
               BRp    MultLoop
;
               JSR    RangeCheck
               ADD    R5,R5,#0      ; R5 contains success/failure
               BRp    Restore2
;
               ADD    R3,R3,#0      ; Test for negative multiplier
               BRz    PushMult
               NOT    R0,R0         ; Adjust for 
               ADD    R0,R0,#1      ; sign of result
PushMult       JSR    PUSH          ; Push product on the stack.
               RET
Restore2       ADD    R6,R6,#-1     ; Adjust stack pointer.
Restore1       ADD    R6,R6,#-1     ; Adjust stack pointer.
Exit           RET

;    Algorithm to pop the top of the stack, form its negative,
;    and push the result on the stack.
;
OpNeg        JSR    POP         ; Get the source operand
             ADD    R5,R5,#0    ; test for successful pop
             BRp    Exit        ; Branch if failure
             NOT    R0,R0
             ADD    R0,R0,#1    ; Form the negative of the source.
             JSR    PUSH        ; Push the result on the stack.
Exit         RET

TRAP   x23         ; Input from the keyboard.
ADD    R1,R0,#0    ; Make room for another input.
TRAP   x23         ; Input another character.
ADD    R0,R1,R0    ; Add the two inputs.
TRAP   x21         ; Display result on the monitor.
TRAP   x25         ; Halt.

;
;  This algorithm takes an ASCII string of three decimal digits and 
;  converts it into a binary number.  R0 is used to collect the result.
;  R1 keeps track of how many digits are left to process.  ASCIIBUFF
;  contains the most significant digit in the ASCII string.
;
ASCIItoBinary  AND    R0,R0,#0      ; R0 will be used for our result 
               ADD    R1,R1,#0      ; Test number of digits.
               BRz    DoneAtoB      ; There are no digits
;
               LD     R3,NegASCIIOffset  ; R3 gets xFFD0, i.e., -x0030
               LEA    R2,ASCIIBUFF
               ADD    R2,R2,R1
               ADD    R2,R2,#-1     ; R2 now points to "ones" digit
;              
               LDR    R4,R2,#0      ; R4 <-- "ones" digit
               ADD    R4,R4,R3      ; Strip off the ASCII template
               ADD    R0,R0,R4      ; Add ones contribution
;
               ADD    R1,R1,#-1
               BRz    DoneAtoB      ; The original number had one digit
               ADD    R2,R2,#-1     ; R2  now points to "tens" digit
;
               LDR    R4,R2,#0      ; R4 <-- "tens" digit
               ADD    R4,R4,R3      ; Strip off ASCII  template
               LEA    R5,LookUp10   ; LookUp10 is BASE of tens values
               ADD    R5,R5,R4      ; R5 points to the right tens value
               LDR    R4,R5,#0
               ADD    R0,R0,R4      ; Add tens contribution to total
;
               ADD    R1,R1,#-1
               BRz    DoneAtoB      ; The original number had two digits
               ADD    R2,R2,#-1     ; R2 now points to "hundreds" digit
;        
               LDR    R4,R2,#0      ; R4 <-- "hundreds" digit
               ADD    R4,R4,R3      ; Strip off ASCII template
               LEA    R5,LookUp100  ; LookUp100 is hundreds BASE
               ADD    R5,R5,R4      ; R5 points to hundreds value
               LDR    R4,R5,#0
               ADD    R0,R0,R4      ; Add hundreds contribution to total
;         
DoneAtoB       RET
NegASCIIOffset .FILL  xFFD0
ASCIIBUFF      .BLKW  4
LookUp10       .FILL  #0
               .FILL  #10
               .FILL  #20
               .FILL  #30
               .FILL  #40
               .FILL  #50
               .FILL  #60
               .FILL  #70
               .FILL  #80
               .FILL  #90
;
LookUp100      .FILL  #0
               .FILL  #100
               .FILL  #200
               .FILL  #300
               .FILL  #400
               .FILL  #500
               .FILL  #600
               .FILL  #700
               .FILL  #800
               .FILL  #900

;
;  This algorithm takes the 2's complement representation of a signed
;  integer, within the range -999 to +999, and converts it into an ASCII 
;  string consisting of a sign digit, followed by three decimal digits.  
;  R0 contains the initial value being converted.
;
BinarytoASCII  LEA   R1,ASCIIBUFF  ; R1 points to string being generated
               ADD   R0,R0,#0      ; R0 contains the binary value
               BRn   NegSign       ; 
               LD    R2,ASCIIplus  ; First store the ASCII plus sign
               STR   R2,R1,#0
               BRnzp Begin100
NegSign        LD    R2,ASCIIminus ; First store ASCII minus sign
               STR   R2,R1,#0
               NOT   R0,R0         ; Convert the number to absolute
               ADD   R0,R0,#1      ; value; it is easier to work with.
;
Begin100       LD    R2,ASCIIoffset ; Prepare for "hundreds" digit
;
               LD    R3,Neg100     ; Determine the hundreds digit
Loop100        ADD   R0,R0,R3
               BRn   End100
               ADD   R2,R2,#1
               BRnzp Loop100
;
End100         STR    R2,R1,#1   ; Store ASCII code for hundreds digit
               LD     R3,Pos100
               ADD    R0,R0,R3   ; Correct R0 for one-too-many subtracts
;
               LD     R2,ASCIIoffset ; Prepare for "tens" digit
;
Begin10        LD     R3,Neg10   ; Determine the tens digit
Loop10         ADD    R0,R0,R3
               BRn    End10
               ADD    R2,R2,#1
               BRnzp  Loop10
;
End10          STR    R2,R1,#2   ; Store ASCII code for tens digit 
               ADD    R0,R0,#10  ; Correct R0 for one-too-many subtracts
Begin1         LD     R2,ASCIIoffset ; Prepare for "ones" digit
               ADD    R2,R2,R0
               STR    R2,R1,#3
               RET
;
ASCIIplus      .FILL  x002B
ASCIIminus     .FILL  x002D
ASCIIoffset    .FILL  x0030
Neg100         .FILL  xFF9C
Pos100         .FILL  x0064
Neg10          .FILL  xFFF6

;
;  The Calculator, Main Algorithm
;
                     LEA       R6,StackBase  ; Initialize the Stack.
                     ADD       R6,R6,#1     ; R6 is stack pointer
                     LEA       R0,PromptMsg
                     PUTS
                     GETC
                     OUT
;                                          
; Check the command
;
Test                 LD        R1,NegX       ; Check for X   
                     ADD       R1,R1,R0
                     BRz       Exit
;
                     LD        R1,NegC       ; Check for C   
                     ADD       R1,R1,R0
                     BRz       OpClear       ; See Figure 10.27
;
                     LD        R1,NegPlus    ; Check for +   
                     ADD       R1,R1,R0
                     BRz       OpAdd         ; See Figure 10.10
;
                     LD        R1,NegMult    ; Check for *   
                     ADD       R1,R1,R0
                     BRz       OpMult        ; See Figure 10.14
;
                     LD        R1,NegMinus   ; Check for -   
                     ADD       R1,R1,R0
                     BRz       OpNeg         ; See Figure 10.15
;
                     LD        R1,NegD       ; Check for D   
                     ADD       R1,R1,R0
                     BRz       OpDisplay     ; See Figure 10.26
;
; Then we must be entering an integer
;
                     BRnzp     PushValue     ; See Figure 10.23
;
NewCommand           LEA       R0,PromptMsg
                     PUTS
                     GETC
                     OUT
                     BRnzp     Test
Exit                 HALT
PromptMsg            .FILL     x000A
                     .STRINGZ "Enter a command:"
NegX                 .FILL     xFFA8
NegC                 .FILL     xFFBD
NegPlus              .FILL     xFFD5
NegMinus             .FILL     xFFD3
NegMult              .FILL     xFFD6
NegD                 .FILL     xFFBC

; This algorithm takes a sequence of ASCII digits typed by the user, 
; converts it into a binary value by calling the ASCIItoBinary 
; subroutine and pushes the binary value onto the stack.  
; 
PushValue         LEA       R1,ASCIIBUFF  ; R1 points to string being 
                  LD        R2,MaxDigits  ; generated
;
ValueLoop         ADD       R3,R0,xFFF6   ; Test for carriage return
                  BRz       GoodInput
                  ADD       R2,R2,#0
                  BRz       TooLargeInput
                  ADD       R2,R2,#-1     ; Still room for more digits
                  STR       R0,R1,#0      ; Store last character read
                  ADD       R1,R1,#1     
                  GETC
                  OUT                     ; Echo it
                  BRnzp     ValueLoop
;
GoodInput         LEA       R2,ASCIIBUFF
                  NOT       R2,R2
                  ADD       R2,R2,#1
                  ADD       R1,R1,R2      ; R1 now contains no. of char.
                  JSR       ASCIItoBinary
                  JSR       PUSH
                  BRnzp     NewCommand
;
TooLargeInput     GETC                    ; Spin until carriage return
                  OUT
                  ADD       R3,R0,xFFF6
                  BRnp      TooLargeInput
                  LEA       R0,TooManyDigits
                  PUTS
                  BRnzp     NewCommand
TooManyDigits     .FILL     x000A
                  .STRINGZ "Too many digits"
MaxDigits         .FILL     x0003

;  This algorithm POPs a value from the stack and puts it in
;  R0 before returning to the calling program.  R5 is used to
;  report success (R5=0) or failure (R5=1) of the POP operation.
POP            LEA     R0,StackBase
               NOT     R0,R0
               ADD     R0,R0,#1       ; R0 = -addr.ofStackBase 
               ADD     R0,R0,R6       ; R6 = StackPointer
               BRz     Underflow
               LDR     R0,R6,#0       ; The actual POP 
               ADD     R6,R6,#1      ; Adjust StackPointer
               AND     R5,R5,#0       ; R5 <-- success
               RET 
Underflow      ST      R7,Save        ; TRAP/RET needs R7
               LEA     R0,UnderflowMsg
               PUTS                   ; Print error message.
               LD      R7,Save        ; Restore R7
               AND     R5,R5,#0  
               ADD     R5,R5,#1       ; R5 <-- failure
               RET
Save           .FILL   x0000
StackMax       .BLKW   9
StackBase      .FILL   x0000
UnderflowMsg   .FILL   x000A
               .STRINGZ "Error: Too Few Values on the Stack."

;  This algorithm PUSHes on the stack the value stored in R0.
;  R5 is used to report success (R5=0) or failure (R5=1) of 
;  the PUSH operation.
;
PUSH           ST      R1,Save1      ; R1 is needed by this routine
               LEA     R1,StackMax
               NOT     R1,R1
               ADD     R1,R1,#1      ; R1 = - addr. of StackMax    
               ADD     R1,R1,R6      ; R6 = StackPointer
               BRz     Overflow
               ADD     R6,R6,#-1     ; Adjust StackPointer for PUSH
               STR     R0,R6,#0      ; The actual PUSH
               BRnzp   Success_exit
Overflow       ST      R7,Save
               LEA     R0,OverflowMsg
               PUTS
               LD      R7,Save
               LD      R1, Save1     ; Restore R1
               AND     R5,R5,#0    
               ADD     R5,R5,#1      ; R5 <-- failure
               RET
Success_exit   LD      R1,Save1      ; Restore R1
               AND     R5,R5,#0      ; R5 <-- success
               RET
Save           .FILL   x0000
Save1          .FILL   x0000
OverflowMsg    .STRINGZ "Error: Stack is Full."

; This algorithm calls BinarytoASCII to convert the 2's complement 
; number on the top of the stack into an ASCII character string, and
; then calls PUTS to display that number on the screen.
OpDisplay    JSR       POP         ; R0 gets the value to be displayed
             ADD       R5,R5,#0  
             BRp       NewCommand  ; POP failed, nothing on the stack.
             JSR       BinarytoASCII 
             LD        R0,NewlineChar
             OUT
             LEA       R0,ASCIIBUFF
             PUTS
             ADD       R6,R6,#-1   ; Push displayed number back on stack
             BRnzp     NewCommand
NewlineChar  .FILL     x000A

;
; Subroutines for carrying out the PUSH and POP functions.  This 
; program works with a stack consisting of memory locations x3FFF
; (BASE) through x3FFB (MAX).  R6 is the stack pointer.
;
POP            ST      R2,Save2       ; are needed by POP.
               ST      R1,Save1
               LD      R1,BASE        ; BASE contains -x3FFF.
               ADD     R1,R1,#-1      ; R1 contains -x4000.
               ADD     R2,R6,R1       ; Compare stack pointer to x4000
               BRz     fail_exit      ; Branch if stack is empty.
               LDR     R0,R6,#0       ; The actual "pop."
               ADD     R6,R6,#1       ; Adjust stack pointer
               BRnzp   success_exit
PUSH           ST      R2,Save2       ; Save registers that 
               ST      R1,Save1       ; are needed by PUSH.
               LD      R1,MAX         ; MAX contains -x3FFB
               ADD     R2,R6,R1       ; Compare stack pointer to -x3FFB
               BRz     fail_exit      ; Branch if stack is full.
               ADD     R6,R6,#-1      ; Adjust stack pointer
               STR     R0,R6,#0       ; The actual "push"
success_exit   LD      R1,Save1       ; Restore original 
               LD      R2,Save2       ; register values.
               AND     R5,R5,#0       ; R5 <-- success.
               RET
fail_exit      LD      R1,Save1       ; Restore original
               LD      R2,Save2       ; register values.
               AND     R5,R5,#0      
               ADD     R5,R5,#1       ; R5 <-- failure. 
               RET
BASE           .FILL   xC001          ; BASE contains -x3FFF.
MAX            .FILL   xC005
Save1          .FILL   x0000
Save2          .FILL   x0000

turns.content: Now I have a thorough understanding of all the reference code. Let me implement the complete LC-3 stack calculator. I need to handle: X, C, +, *, %, @ (XOR), neg, D commands, and digit pushing.

tool_calls.name: write
tool_calls.arguments:
{"file_path": "/tmp/isolated_workspace_1091/lab2.asm", "content": "; ============================================================
; LC-3 Stack-Based Calculator
; Commands: X (exit), C (clear), + (add), * (mult),
;           % (mod), @ (XOR), neg (negate), D (display),
;           digits (push integer)
; Stack: x3FFF (base/empty) down to x3FFB (max/full)
; R6 = stack pointer (points to top element)
; R5 = 0 success, R5 = 1 failure for PUSH/POP/RangeCheck
; ============================================================
        .ORIG   x3000

; ============================================================
; MAIN PROGRAM - Initialization
; ============================================================
        LEA     R6,StackBase    ; R6 points just past StackBase
        ADD     R6,R6,#1        ; R6 = StackBase address + 1 = x3FFF (empty stack)

NewCommand
        LEA     R0,PromptMsg
        PUTS
        GETC
        OUT

; ============================================================
; Command Dispatch
; ============================================================
Test
        LD      R1,NegX         ; Check for 'X' (x58)
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     ExitProg

        LD      R1,NegC         ; Check for 'C' (x43)
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpClear

        LD      R1,NegPlus      ; Check for '+' (x2B)
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpAdd

        LD      R1,NegMult      ; Check for '*' (x2A)
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpMult

        LD      R1,NegPercent   ; Check for '%' (x25)
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpMod

        LD      R1,NegAt        ; Check for '@' (x40)
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpXOR

        LD      R1,NegN         ; Check for 'n' (start of "neg") (x6E)
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     ReadNeg

        LD      R1,NegD         ; Check for 'D' (x44)
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpDisplay

        ; Check if it's a digit (0-9) or '-' (negative number)
        LD      R1,NegMinus     ; Check for '-'
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     PushValue       ; negative number starts with '-'

        LD      R1,NegZero      ; Check >= '0' (x30)
        ADD     R2,R1,R0        ; R2 = R0 - 0x30
        BRn     NewCommand      ; below '0', skip

        LD      R1,NegNine      ; Check <= '9' (x39)
        ADD     R2,R1,R0        ; R2 = R0 - 0x39  (should be BRnz for <= 9)
        BRp     NewCommand      ; above '9', skip

        ; It's a digit
        BRnzp   PushValue

; ============================================================
; ReadNeg: read 'e','g' to confirm "neg" command
; ============================================================
ReadNeg
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R1,NegE         ; Check for 'e'
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRnp    NewCommand      ; not 'e', skip

        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R1,NegG         ; Check for 'g'
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRnp    NewCommand      ; not 'g', skip

        BRnzp   OpNeg

ExitProg
        HALT

; ============================================================
; OpClear - Clear the stack
; ============================================================
OpClear
        LEA     R6,StackBase
        ADD     R6,R6,#1        ; Reset stack pointer to base (empty)
        BRnzp   NewCommand

; ============================================================
; OpAdd - Pop two values, add, range check, push
; ============================================================
OpAdd
        ST      R7,SaveR7OpAdd
        JSR     POP             ; First operand -> R0
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpAddExit       ; POP failed
        ADD     R1,R0,#0        ; Save first operand in R1
        JSR     POP             ; Second operand -> R0
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpAddRestore1   ; POP failed, restore first
        ADD     R0,R0,R1        ; R0 = second + first
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpAddRestore2   ; Out of range
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpAdd
        BRnzp   NewCommand
OpAddRestore2
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1       ; Restore second operand slot
OpAddRestore1
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1       ; Restore first operand slot
OpAddExit
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpAdd
        BRnzp   NewCommand
SaveR7OpAdd .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpMult - Pop two values, multiply, range check, push
; ============================================================
OpMult
        ST      R7,SaveR7OpMult
        AND     R3,R3,#0        ; R3 = sign flag (0=pos, 1=neg)
        JSR     POP             ; First operand -> R0
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpMultExit
        ADD     R1,R0,#0        ; R1 = multiplicand
        JSR     POP             ; Second operand -> R0 (multiplier)
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpMultRestore1
        ADD     R2,R0,#0        ; R2 = multiplier, test sign
        BRzp    MultPosMultiplier
        ADD     R3,R3,#1        ; Flag: multiplier is negative
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1        ; R2 = |multiplier|
MultPosMultiplier
        AND     R0,R0,#0        ; Clear product
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     MultDoPush      ; Multiplier = 0, product = 0
MultLoop
        ADD     R0,R0,R1        ; Accumulate
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
        BRp     MultLoop
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpMultRestore2
        ADD     R3,R3,#0        ; Adjust sign if needed
        BRz     MultDoPush
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
MultDoPush
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMult
        BRnzp   NewCommand
OpMultRestore2
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpMultRestore1
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpMultExit
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMult
        BRnzp   NewCommand
SaveR7OpMult .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpMod - Pop two values, compute remainder (a % b = a - b*(a/b))
; Uses repeated subtraction: result = a - (a/b)*b
; Convention: a is top (popped first), b is second
;   We compute a % b = a - floor(a/b)*b
;   For simplicity: result has same sign as dividend (a)
; ============================================================
OpMod
        ST      R7,SaveR7OpMod
        JSR     POP             ; a (dividend) -> R0
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpModExit
        ADD     R1,R0,#0        ; R1 = a (dividend)
        JSR     POP             ; b (divisor) -> R0
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpModRestore1
        ADD     R2,R0,#0        ; R2 = b

        ; Check for division by zero
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     OpModDivZero

        ; Compute |a| and |b|, track signs
        AND     R3,R3,#0        ; R3 = sign of result (0=pos)
        ADD     R4,R1,#0        ; R4 = a (will use for computation)
        BRzp    ModAPos
        ADD     R3,R3,#1        ; a is negative, result sign = neg
        NOT     R4,R4
        ADD     R4,R4,#1        ; R4 = |a|
ModAPos
        ADD     R2,R2,#0        ; R2 = b
        BRzp    ModBPos
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1        ; R2 = |b|
ModBPos
        ; Now compute |a| mod |b| by repeated subtraction
        ; R4 = |a|, R2 = |b|
ModSubLoop
        NOT     R0,R2
        ADD     R0,R0,#1        ; R0 = -|b|
        ADD     R0,R4,R0        ; R0 = |a| - |b|
        BRn     ModDone         ; |a| < |b|, remainder = |a|
        ADD     R4,R0,#0        ; |a| = remainder
        BRnzp   ModSubLoop
ModDone
        ADD     R0,R4,#0        ; R0 = |a| mod |b|
        ; Apply sign of original dividend
        ADD     R3,R3,#0
        BRz     ModSignOK
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1        ; Negate result
ModSignOK
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpModRestore2
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMod
        BRnzp   NewCommand

OpModDivZero
        LEA     R0,DivZeroMsg
        PUTS
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1       ; Restore a
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMod
        BRnzp   NewCommand

OpModRestore2
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpModRestore1
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpModExit
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMod
        BRnzp   NewCommand
SaveR7OpMod .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpXOR - Pop two values, XOR them, push result
; XOR via: a XOR b = (a OR b) AND NOT(a AND b)
; Or bit by bit: NOT((NOT a) AND (NOT b)) AND NOT(a AND b)
