; Simpler: a XOR b = (NOT a AND b) OR (a AND NOT b)
;   In LC-3 using AND/NOT:
;   a XOR b = NOT((NOT(NOT a AND b)) AND (NOT(a AND NOT b)))
; ============================================================
OpXOR
        ST      R7,SaveR7OpXOR
        JSR     POP             ; a -> R0
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpXORExit
        ADD     R1,R0,#0        ; R1 = a
        JSR     POP             ; b -> R0
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpXORRestore1
        ADD     R2,R0,#0        ; R2 = b
        ; XOR: a XOR b = (NOT a AND b) OR (a AND NOT b)
        ; = NOT( NOT(NOT a AND b) AND NOT(a AND NOT b) )
        NOT     R3,R1           ; R3 = NOT a
        AND     R3,R3,R2        ; R3 = NOT a AND b
        NOT     R4,R2           ; R4 = NOT b
        AND     R4,R1,R4        ; R4 = a AND NOT b
        ; R3 OR R4 = NOT(NOT R3 AND NOT R4)
        NOT     R3,R3           ; NOT(NOT a AND b)
        NOT     R4,R4           ; NOT(a AND NOT b)
        AND     R0,R3,R4        ; NOT(NOT a AND b) AND NOT(a AND NOT b)
        NOT     R0,R0           ; Final XOR result
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpXORRestore2
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpXOR
        BRnzp   NewCommand
OpXORRestore2
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpXORRestore1
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpXORExit
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpXOR
        BRnzp   NewCommand
SaveR7OpXOR .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpNeg - Pop one value, negate it, push result
; ============================================================
OpNeg
        ST      R7,SaveR7OpNeg
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpNegExit
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1        ; R0 = -R0
        JSR     PUSH
OpNegExit
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpNeg
        BRnzp   NewCommand
SaveR7OpNeg .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpDisplay - Display top of stack without popping
; ============================================================
OpDisplay
        ST      R7,SaveR7OpDisp
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpDispExit      ; Stack empty
        ST      R0,DispSaveVal  ; Save value
        JSR     BinarytoASCII   ; Convert to ASCII string in ASCIIBUFF
        LD      R0,NewlineChar
        OUT
        LEA     R0,ASCIIBUFF
        PUTS
        ; Push value back (we popped it just to peek)
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1       ; Restore stack pointer (undo the POP)
OpDispExit
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpDisp
        BRnzp   NewCommand
SaveR7OpDisp .FILL x0000
DispSaveVal  .FILL x0000
NewlineChar  .FILL x000A

; ============================================================
; PushValue - Read digits (possibly with leading '-'), convert
; to binary, and push onto stack.
; R0 holds the first character already read.
; ============================================================
PushValue
        ST      R7,SaveR7Push
        LEA     R1,ASCIIBUFF    ; R1 = pointer into buffer
        LD      R2,MaxDigits    ; R2 = max digits counter

        ; Check if first char is '-' (negative number)
        AND     R4,R4,#0        ; R4 = negative flag
        LD      R3,NegMinus2
        ADD     R3,R3,R0
        BRnp    PVNotMinus
        ADD     R4,R4,#1        ; Set negative flag
        ; Read next character (first actual digit)
        GETC
        OUT
PVNotMinus
        ; Store first digit character
        STR     R0,R1,#0
        ADD     R1,R1,#1
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1

PVLoop
        GETC
        OUT
        ; Test for carriage return (x000D) or newline (x000A)
        ADD     R3,R0,#-13      ; Test CR
        BRz     PVGoodInput
        ADD     R3,R0,#-10      ; Test LF/newline
        BRz     PVGoodInput
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     PVTooLarge      ; No more room
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
        STR     R0,R1,#0
        ADD     R1,R1,#1
        BRnzp   PVLoop

PVGoodInput
        ; Compute number of digits: R1 - ASCIIBUFF
        LEA     R2,ASCIIBUFF
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        ADD     R1,R1,R2        ; R1 = number of digits stored
        JSR     ASCIItoBinary   ; Result in R0
        ; Apply negative flag
        ADD     R4,R4,#0
        BRz     PVPush
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1        ; Negate
PVPush
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7Push
        BRnzp   NewCommand

PVTooLarge
        ; Spin until end of line
PVFlushLoop
        GETC
        OUT
        ADD     R3,R0,#-13
        BRz     PVFlushDone
        ADD     R3,R0,#-10
        BRnp    PVFlushLoop
PVFlushDone
        LEA     R0,TooManyDigits
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Push
        BRnzp   NewCommand
SaveR7Push .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; PUSH subroutine
; Input: R0 = value to push
; Output: R5 = 0 (success), R5 = 1 (failure/full)
; Modifies: R6 (stack pointer)
; ============================================================
PUSH
        ST      R1,PushSave1
        ST      R7,PushSaveR7
        LEA     R1,StackMax
        NOT     R1,R1
        ADD     R1,R1,#1        ; R1 = -addr(StackMax)
        ADD     R1,R1,R6        ; Compare R6 to StackMax addr
        BRz     PushOverflow
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1       ; Adjust stack pointer
        STR     R0,R6,#0        ; Store value
        LD      R1,PushSave1
        LD      R7,PushSaveR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0        ; R5 = success
        RET
PushOverflow
        ST      R7,PushSaveR7B
        LEA     R0,OverflowMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,PushSaveR7B
        LD      R1,PushSave1
        LD      R7,PushSaveR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1        ; R5 = failure
        RET
PushSave1   .FILL x0000
PushSaveR7  .FILL x0000
PushSaveR7B .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; POP subroutine
; Output: R0 = popped value, R5 = 0 (success), R5 = 1 (failure/empty)
; Modifies: R6 (stack pointer)
; ============================================================
POP
        ST      R1,PopSave1
        ST      R7,PopSaveR7
        LEA     R1,StackBase
        NOT     R1,R1
        ADD     R1,R1,#1        ; R1 = -addr(StackBase)
        ADD     R1,R1,R6        ; R6 - StackBase: 0 means empty
        BRz     PopUnderflow
        LDR     R0,R6,#0        ; Load top value
        ADD     R6,R6,#1        ; Adjust stack pointer
        LD      R1,PopSave1
        LD      R7,PopSaveR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0        ; R5 = success
        RET
PopUnderflow
        ST      R7,PopSaveR7B
        LEA     R0,UnderflowMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,PopSaveR7B
        LD      R1,PopSave1
        LD      R7,PopSaveR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1        ; R5 = failure
        RET
PopSave1   .FILL x0000
PopSaveR7  .FILL x0000
PopSaveR7B .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; RangeCheck subroutine
; Input: R0 = value to check
; Output: R5 = 0 if in [-999,999], R5 = 1 if out of range
; Preserves: R0
; ============================================================
RangeCheck
        ST      R7,RCSaveR7
        LD      R4,Neg999
        ADD     R3,R0,R4        ; R0 + (-999): if negative, R0 < -999
        BRp     RCBadRange      ; R0 - (-999) > 0 means R0 > 999? No wait...
        ; Wait: R0 + (-999): if R0 = -999, result = -1998, BRn -> OK (just checking >=)
        ; Let me redo: to check R0 >= -999: compute R0 - (-999) = R0 + 999
        ; if that is negative, R0 < -999 (bad)
        LD      R4,Pos999       ; R4 = 999
        ADD     R3,R0,R4        ; R3 = R0 + 999; if negative, R0 < -999
        BRn     RCBadRange
        LD      R4,Neg999       ; R4 = -999
        NOT     R4,R4
        ADD     R4,R4,#1        ; R4 = 999
        ; Check R0 <= 999: compute 999 - R0; if negative, R0 > 999
        NOT     R3,R0
        ADD     R3,R3,#1        ; R3 = -R0
        ADD     R3,R3,R4        ; R3 = 999 - R0; if negative, R0 > 999
        BRn     RCBadRange
        LD      R7,RCSaveR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0        ; Success
        RET
RCBadRange
        ST      R7,RCSaveR7B
        LEA     R0,RangeErrMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,RCSaveR7B
        LD      R0,RCSaveR0     ; Hmm, we didn't save R0 yet...
        LD      R7,RCSaveR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1        ; Failure
        RET
RCSaveR7  .FILL x0000
RCSaveR7B .FILL x0000
RCSaveR0  .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; ASCIItoBinary subroutine
; Input:  ASCIIBUFF = ASCII digit characters, R1 = count of digits
; Output: R0 = binary value
; ============================================================
ASCIItoBinary
        AND     R0,R0,#0        ; Clear result
        ADD     R1,R1,#0
        BRz     DoneAtoB
        LD      R3,NegASCIIOffset   ; R3 = -x30 (strip ASCII)
        LEA     R2,ASCIIBUFF
        ADD     R2,R2,R1
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1       ; R2 points to ones digit
        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3        ; Ones digit value
        ADD     R0,R0,R4
        ADD     R1,R1,#-1
        BRz     DoneAtoB
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1       ; Point to tens digit
        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3        ; Tens digit value
        LEA     R5,LookUp10
        ADD     R5,R5,R4
        LDR     R4,R5,#0
        ADD     R0,R0,R4
        ADD     R1,R1,#-1
        BRz     DoneAtoB
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1       ; Point to hundreds digit
        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3
        LEA     R5,LookUp100
        ADD     R5,R5,R4
        LDR     R4,R5,#0
        ADD     R0,R0,R4
DoneAtoB
        RET

; ============================================================
; BinarytoASCII subroutine
; Input: R0 = binary value in [-999, 999]
; Output: ASCIIBUFF = 4-char ASCII string (sign + 3 digits) + null
; ============================================================
BinarytoASCII
        ST      R7,BtASaveR7
        LEA     R1,ASCIIBUFF
        ADD     R0,R0,#0
        BRn     BtANeg
        LD      R2,ASCIIplus
        STR     R2,R1,#0
        BRnzp   BtAHundreds
BtANeg
        LD      R2,ASCIIminus
        STR     R2,R1,#0
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1        ; R0 = |value|
BtAHundreds
        LD      R2,ASCIIoffset  ; R2 = '0'
        LD      R3,Neg100
BtALoop100
        ADD     R0,R0,R3        ; R0 -= 100
        BRn     BtAEnd100
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        BRnzp   BtALoop100
BtAEnd100
        STR     R2,R1,#1        ; Store hundreds ASCII
        LD      R3,Pos100
        ADD     R0,R0,R3        ; Correct for overshoot
        LD      R2,ASCIIoffset
        LD      R3,Neg10
BtALoop10
        ADD     R0,R0,R3
        BRn     BtAEnd10
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        BRnzp   BtALoop10
BtAEnd10
        STR     R2,R1,#2
        ADD     R0,R0,#10       ; Correct for overshoot
        LD      R2,ASCIIoffset
        ADD     R2,R2,R0        ; Ones digit
        STR     R2,R1,#3
        ; Null-terminate
        AND     R2,R2,#0
        STR     R2,R1,#4
        LD      R7,BtASaveR7
        RET
BtASaveR7 .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; Data Section
; ============================================================

; --- Stack ---
StackMax   .BLKW  5             ; 5 slots (x3FFB to x3FFF)
StackBase  .FILL  x0000         ; Sentinel (stack empty when R6 = addr+1)

; --- ASCII Buffer (4 chars + null for display, up to 3 digits for input) ---
ASCIIBUFF  .BLKW  6

; --- Prompt ---
PromptMsg  .FILL  x000A
           .STRINGZ "Enter a command: "

; --- Error messages ---
OverflowMsg  .FILL x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Stack is Full."
UnderflowMsg .FILL x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Too Few Values on the Stack."
RangeErrMsg  .FILL x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Number is out of range."
DivZeroMsg   .FILL x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Division by zero."
TooManyDigits .FILL x000A
              .STRINGZ "Too many digits"

; --- Constants for dispatch ---
NegX       .FILL  xFFA8         ; -'X' = -x58
NegC       .FILL  xFFBD         ; -'C' = -x43
NegPlus    .FILL  xFFD5         ; -'+' = -x2B
NegMult    .FILL  xFFD6         ; -'*' = -x2A
NegPercent .FILL  xFFDB         ; -'%' = -x25
NegAt      .FILL  xFFC0         ; -'@' = -x40
NegN       .FILL  xFF92         ; -'n' = -x6E
NegD       .FILL  xFFBC         ; -'D' = -x44
NegMinus   .FILL  xFFD3         ; -'-' = -x2D
NegMinus2  .FILL  xFFD3         ; -'-' (second copy for PushValue)
NegZero    .FILL  xFFD0         ; -'0' = -x30
NegNine    .FILL  xFFC7         ; -'9' = -x39
NegE       .FILL  xFF9B         ; -'e' = -x65
NegG       .FILL  xFF99         ; -'g' = -x67

; --- Range check constants ---
Pos999     .FILL  #999
Neg999     .FILL  #-999

; --- ASCII conversion constants ---
NegASCIIOffset .FILL xFFD0      ; -x30
ASCIIplus      .FILL x002B
ASCIIminus     .FILL x002D
ASCIIoffset    .FILL x0030
Neg100         .FILL xFF9C      ; -100
Pos100         .FILL x0064      ; +100
Neg10          .FILL xFFF6      ; -10

; --- Lookup tables for ASCIItoBinary ---
LookUp10   .FILL  #0
           .FILL  #10
           .FILL  #20
           .FILL  #30
           .FILL  #40
           .FILL  #50
           .FILL  #60
           .FILL  #70
           .FILL  #80
           .FILL  #90

LookUp100  .FILL  #0
           .FILL  #100
           .FILL  #200
           .FILL  #300
           .FILL  #400
           .FILL  #500
           .FILL  #600
           .FILL  #700
           .FILL  #800
           .FILL  #900

MaxDigits  .FILL  #3

        .END
"}

tool_results.content: Successfully wrote 21970 bytes to /tmp/isolated_workspace_1091/lab2.asm

turns.content: Now let me review the code carefully for bugs — especially the RangeCheck, the stack layout, and the dispatch logic:

tool_results.content:
; ============================================================
; LC-3 Stack-Based Calculator
; Commands: X (exit), C (clear), + (add), * (mult),
;           % (mod), @ (XOR), neg (negate), D (display),
;           digits (push integer)
; Stack: x3FFF (base/empty) down to x3FFB (max/full)
; R6 = stack pointer (points to top element)
; R5 = 0 success, R5 = 1 failure for PUSH/POP/RangeCheck
; ============================================================
        .ORIG   x3000

; ============================================================
; MAIN PROGRAM - Initialization
; ============================================================
        LEA     R6,StackBase    ; R6 points just past StackBase
        ADD     R6,R6,#1        ; R6 = StackBase address + 1 = x3FFF (empty stack)

NewCommand
        LEA     R0,PromptMsg
        PUTS
        GETC
        OUT

; ============================================================
; Command Dispatch
; ============================================================
Test
        LD      R1,NegX         ; Check for 'X' (x58)
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     ExitProg

        LD      R1,NegC         ; Check for 'C' (x43)
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpClear

        LD      R1,NegPlus      ; Check for '+' (x2B)
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpAdd

        LD      R1,NegMult      ; Check for '*' (x2A)
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpMult

        LD      R1,NegPercent   ; Check for '%' (x25)
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpMod

        LD      R1,NegAt        ; Check for '@' (x40)
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpXOR

        LD      R1,NegN         ; Check for 'n' (start of "neg") (x6E)
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     ReadNeg

        LD      R1,NegD         ; Check for 'D' (x44)
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpDisplay

        ; Check if it's a digit (0-9) or '-' (negative number)
        LD      R1,NegMinus     ; Check for '-'
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     PushValue       ; negative number starts with '-'

        LD      R1,NegZero      ; Check >= '0' (x30)
        ADD     R2,R1,R0        ; R2 = R0 - 0x30
        BRn     NewCommand      ; below '0', skip

        LD      R1,NegNine      ; Check <= '9' (x39)
        ADD     R2,R1,R0        ; R2 = R0 - 0x39  (should be BRnz for <= 9)
        BRp     NewCommand      ; above '9', skip

        ; It's a digit
        BRnzp   PushValue

; ============================================================
; ReadNeg: read 'e','g' to confirm "neg" command
; ============================================================
ReadNeg
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R1,NegE         ; Check for 'e'
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRnp    NewCommand      ; not 'e', skip

        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R1,NegG         ; Check for 'g'
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRnp    NewCommand      ; not 'g', skip

        BRnzp   OpNeg

ExitProg
        HALT

; ============================================================
; OpClear - Clear the stack
; ============================================================
OpClear
        LEA     R6,StackBase
        ADD     R6,R6,#1        ; Reset stack pointer to base (empty)
        BRnzp   NewCommand

; ============================================================
; OpAdd - Pop two values, add, range check, push
; ============================================================
OpAdd
        ST      R7,SaveR7OpAdd
        JSR     POP             ; First operand -> R0
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpAddExit       ; POP failed
        ADD     R1,R0,#0        ; Save first operand in R1
        JSR     POP             ; Second operand -> R0
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpAddRestore1   ; POP failed, restore first
        ADD     R0,R0,R1        ; R0 = second + first
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpAddRestore2   ; Out of range
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpAdd
        BRnzp   NewCommand
OpAddRestore2
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1       ; Restore second operand slot
OpAddRestore1
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1       ; Restore first operand slot
OpAddExit
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpAdd
        BRnzp   NewCommand
SaveR7OpAdd .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpMult - Pop two values, multiply, range check, push
; ============================================================
OpMult
        ST      R7,SaveR7OpMult
        AND     R3,R3,#0        ; R3 = sign flag (0=pos, 1=neg)
        JSR     POP             ; First operand -> R0
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpMultExit
        ADD     R1,R0,#0        ; R1 = multiplicand
        JSR     POP             ; Second operand -> R0 (multiplier)
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpMultRestore1
        ADD     R2,R0,#0        ; R2 = multiplier, test sign
        BRzp    MultPosMultiplier
        ADD     R3,R3,#1        ; Flag: multiplier is negative
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1        ; R2 = |multiplier|
MultPosMultiplier
        AND     R0,R0,#0        ; Clear product
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     MultDoPush      ; Multiplier = 0, product = 0
MultLoop
        ADD     R0,R0,R1        ; Accumulate
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
        BRp     MultLoop
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpMultRestore2
        ADD     R3,R3,#0        ; Adjust sign if needed
        BRz     MultDoPush
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
MultDoPush
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMult
        BRnzp   NewCommand
OpMultRestore2
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpMultRestore1
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpMultExit
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMult
        BRnzp   NewCommand
SaveR7OpMult .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpMod - Pop two values, compute remainder (a % b = a - b*(a/b))
; Uses repeated subtraction: result = a - (a/b)*b
; Convention: a is top (popped first), b is second
;   We compute a % b = a - floor(a/b)*b
;   For simplicity: result has same sign as dividend (a)
; ============================================================
OpMod
        ST      R7,SaveR7OpMod
        JSR     POP             ; a (dividend) -> R0
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpModExit
        ADD     R1,R0,#0        ; R1 = a (dividend)
        JSR     POP             ; b (divisor) -> R0
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpModRestore1
        ADD     R2,R0,#0        ; R2 = b

        ; Check for division by zero
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     OpModDivZero

        ; Compute |a| and |b|, track signs
        AND     R3,R3,#0        ; R3 = sign of result (0=pos)
        ADD     R4,R1,#0        ; R4 = a (will use for computation)
        BRzp    ModAPos
        ADD     R3,R3,#1        ; a is negative, result sign = neg
        NOT     R4,R4
        ADD     R4,R4,#1        ; R4 = |a|
ModAPos
        ADD     R2,R2,#0        ; R2 = b
        BRzp    ModBPos
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1        ; R2 = |b|
ModBPos
        ; Now compute |a| mod |b| by repeated subtraction
        ; R4 = |a|, R2 = |b|
ModSubLoop
        NOT     R0,R2
        ADD     R0,R0,#1        ; R0 = -|b|
        ADD     R0,R4,R0        ; R0 = |a| - |b|
        BRn     ModDone         ; |a| < |b|, remainder = |a|
        ADD     R4,R0,#0        ; |a| = remainder
        BRnzp   ModSubLoop
ModDone
        ADD     R0,R4,#0        ; R0 = |a| mod |b|
        ; Apply sign of original dividend
        ADD     R3,R3,#0
        BRz     ModSignOK
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1        ; Negate result
ModSignOK
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpModRestore2
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMod
        BRnzp   NewCommand

OpModDivZero
        LEA     R0,DivZeroMsg
        PUTS
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1       ; Restore a
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMod
        BRnzp   NewCommand

OpModRestore2
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpModRestore1
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpModExit
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpMod
        BRnzp   NewCommand
SaveR7OpMod .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpXOR - Pop two values, XOR them, push result
; XOR via: a XOR b = (a OR b) AND NOT(a AND b)
; Or bit by bit: NOT((NOT a) AND (NOT b)) AND NOT(a AND b)
; Simpler: a XOR b = (NOT a AND b) OR (a AND NOT b)
;   In LC-3 using AND/NOT:
;   a XOR b = NOT((NOT(NOT a AND b)) AND (NOT(a AND NOT b)))
; ============================================================
OpXOR
        ST      R7,SaveR7OpXOR
        JSR     POP             ; a -> R0
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpXORExit
        ADD     R1,R0,#0        ; R1 = a
        JSR     POP             ; b -> R0
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpXORRestore1
        ADD     R2,R0,#0        ; R2 = b
        ; XOR: a XOR b = (NOT a AND b) OR (a AND NOT b)
        ; = NOT( NOT(NOT a AND b) AND NOT(a AND NOT b) )
        NOT     R3,R1           ; R3 = NOT a
        AND     R3,R3,R2        ; R3 = NOT a AND b
        NOT     R4,R2           ; R4 = NOT b
        AND     R4,R1,R4        ; R4 = a AND NOT b
        ; R3 OR R4 = NOT(NOT R3 AND NOT R4)
        NOT     R3,R3           ; NOT(NOT a AND b)
        NOT     R4,R4           ; NOT(a AND NOT b)
        AND     R0,R3,R4        ; NOT(NOT a AND b) AND NOT(a AND NOT b)
        NOT     R0,R0           ; Final XOR result
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpXORRestore2
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpXOR
        BRnzp   NewCommand
OpXORRestore2
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpXORRestore1
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpXORExit
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpXOR
        BRnzp   NewCommand
SaveR7OpXOR .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpNeg - Pop one value, negate it, push result
; ============================================================
OpNeg
        ST      R7,SaveR7OpNeg
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpNegExit
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1        ; R0 = -R0
        JSR     PUSH
OpNegExit
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpNeg
        BRnzp   NewCommand
SaveR7OpNeg .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; OpDisplay - Display top of stack without popping
; ============================================================
OpDisplay
        ST      R7,SaveR7OpDisp
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpDispExit      ; Stack empty
        ST      R0,DispSaveVal  ; Save value
        JSR     BinarytoASCII   ; Convert to ASCII string in ASCIIBUFF
        LD      R0,NewlineChar
        OUT
        LEA     R0,ASCIIBUFF
        PUTS
        ; Push value back (we popped it just to peek)
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1       ; Restore stack pointer (undo the POP)
OpDispExit
        LD      R7,SaveR7OpDisp
        BRnzp   NewCommand
SaveR7OpDisp .FILL x0000
DispSaveVal  .FILL x0000
NewlineChar  .FILL x000A

; ============================================================
; PushValue - Read digits (possibly with leading '-'), convert
; to binary, and push onto stack.
; R0 holds the first character already read.
; ============================================================
PushValue
        ST      R7,SaveR7Push
        LEA     R1,ASCIIBUFF    ; R1 = pointer into buffer
        LD      R2,MaxDigits    ; R2 = max digits counter

        ; Check if first char is '-' (negative number)
        AND     R4,R4,#0        ; R4 = negative flag
        LD      R3,NegMinus2
        ADD     R3,R3,R0
        BRnp    PVNotMinus
        ADD     R4,R4,#1        ; Set negative flag
        ; Read next character (first actual digit)
        GETC
        OUT
PVNotMinus
        ; Store first digit character
        STR     R0,R1,#0
        ADD     R1,R1,#1
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1

PVLoop
        GETC
        OUT
        ; Test for carriage return (x000D) or newline (x000A)
        ADD     R3,R0,#-13      ; Test CR
        BRz     PVGoodInput
        ADD     R3,R0,#-10      ; Test LF/newline
        BRz     PVGoodInput
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     PVTooLarge      ; No more room
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
        STR     R0,R1,#0
        ADD     R1,R1,#1
        BRnzp   PVLoop

PVGoodInput
        ; Compute number of digits: R1 - ASCIIBUFF
        LEA     R2,ASCIIBUFF
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        ADD     R1,R1,R2        ; R1 = number of digits stored
        JSR     ASCIItoBinary   ; Result in R0
        ; Apply negative flag
        ADD     R4,R4,#0
        BRz     PVPush
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1        ; Negate
PVPush
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7Push
        BRnzp   NewCommand

PVTooLarge
        ; Spin until end of line
PVFlushLoop
        GETC
        OUT
        ADD     R3,R0,#-13
        BRz     PVFlushDone
        ADD     R3,R0,#-10
        BRnp    PVFlushLoop
PVFlushDone
        LEA     R0,TooManyDigits
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7Push
        BRnzp   NewCommand
SaveR7Push .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; PUSH subroutine
; Input: R0 = value to push
; Output: R5 = 0 (success), R5 = 1 (failure/full)
; Modifies: R6 (stack pointer)
; ============================================================
PUSH
        ST      R1,PushSave1
        ST      R7,PushSaveR7
        LEA     R1,StackMax
        NOT     R1,R1
        ADD     R1,R1,#1        ; R1 = -addr(StackMax)
        ADD     R1,R1,R6        ; Compare R6 to StackMax addr
        BRz     PushOverflow
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1       ; Adjust stack pointer
        STR     R0,R6,#0        ; Store value
        LD      R1,PushSave1
        LD      R7,PushSaveR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0        ; R5 = success
        RET
PushOverflow
        ST      R7,PushSaveR7B
        LEA     R0,OverflowMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,PushSaveR7B
        LD      R1,PushSave1
        LD      R7,PushSaveR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1        ; R5 = failure
        RET
PushSave1   .FILL x0000
PushSaveR7  .FILL x0000
PushSaveR7B .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; POP subroutine
; Output: R0 = popped value, R5 = 0 (success), R5 = 1 (failure/empty)
; Modifies: R6 (stack pointer)
; ============================================================
POP
        ST      R1,PopSave1
        ST      R7,PopSaveR7
        LEA     R1,StackBase
        NOT     R1,R1
        ADD     R1,R1,#1        ; R1 = -addr(StackBase)
        ADD     R1,R1,R6        ; R6 - StackBase: 0 means empty
        BRz     PopUnderflow
        LDR     R0,R6,#0        ; Load top value
        ADD     R6,R6,#1        ; Adjust stack pointer
        LD      R1,PopSave1
        LD      R7,PopSaveR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0        ; R5 = success
        RET
PopUnderflow
        ST      R7,PopSaveR7B
        LEA     R0,UnderflowMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,PopSaveR7B
        LD      R1,PopSave1
        LD      R7,PopSaveR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1        ; R5 = failure
        RET
PopSave1   .FILL x0000
PopSaveR7  .FILL x0000
PopSaveR7B .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; RangeCheck subroutine
; Input: R0 = value to check
; Output: R5 = 0 if in [-999,999], R5 = 1 if out of range
; Preserves: R0
; ============================================================
RangeCheck
        ST      R7,RCSaveR7
        LD      R4,Neg999
        ADD     R3,R0,R4        ; R0 + (-999): if negative, R0 < -999
        BRp     RCBadRange      ; R0 - (-999) > 0 means R0 > 999? No wait...
        ; Wait: R0 + (-999): if R0 = -999, result = -1998, BRn -> OK (just checking >=)
        ; Let me redo: to check R0 >= -999: compute R0 - (-999) = R0 + 999
        ; if that is negative, R0 < -999 (bad)
        LD      R4,Pos999       ; R4 = 999
        ADD     R3,R0,R4        ; R3 = R0 + 999; if negative, R0 < -999
        BRn     RCBadRange
        LD      R4,Neg999       ; R4 = -999
        NOT     R4,R4
        ADD     R4,R4,#1        ; R4 = 999
        ; Check R0 <= 999: compute 999 - R0; if negative, R0 > 999
        NOT     R3,R0
        ADD     R3,R3,#1        ; R3 = -R0
        ADD     R3,R3,R4        ; R3 = 999 - R0; if negative, R0 > 999
        BRn     RCBadRange
        LD      R7,RCSaveR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0        ; Success
        RET
RCBadRange
        ST      R7,RCSaveR7B
        LEA     R0,RangeErrMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,RCSaveR7B
        LD      R0,RCSaveR0     ; Hmm, we didn't save R0 yet...
        LD      R7,RCSaveR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1        ; Failure
        RET
RCSaveR7  .FILL x0000
RCSaveR7B .FILL x0000
RCSaveR0  .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; ASCIItoBinary subroutine
; Input:  ASCIIBUFF = ASCII digit characters, R1 = count of digits
; Output: R0 = binary value
; ============================================================
ASCIItoBinary
        AND     R0,R0,#0        ; Clear result
        ADD     R1,R1,#0
        BRz     DoneAtoB
        LD      R3,NegASCIIOffset   ; R3 = -x30 (strip ASCII)
        LEA     R2,ASCIIBUFF
        ADD     R2,R2,R1
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1       ; R2 points to ones digit
        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3        ; Ones digit value
        ADD     R0,R0,R4
        ADD     R1,R1,#-1
        BRz     DoneAtoB
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1       ; Point to tens digit
        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3        ; Tens digit value
        LEA     R5,LookUp10
        ADD     R5,R5,R4
        LDR     R4,R5,#0
        ADD     R0,R0,R4
        ADD     R1,R1,#-1
        BRz     DoneAtoB
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1       ; Point to hundreds digit
        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3
        LEA     R5,LookUp100
        ADD     R5,R5,R4
        LDR     R4,R5,#0
        ADD     R0,R0,R4
DoneAtoB
        RET

; ============================================================
; BinarytoASCII subroutine
; Input: R0 = binary value in [-999, 999]
; Output: ASCIIBUFF = 4-char ASCII string (sign + 3 digits) + null
; ============================================================
BinarytoASCII
        ST      R7,BtASaveR7
        LEA     R1,ASCIIBUFF
        ADD     R0,R0,#0
        BRn     BtANeg
        LD      R2,ASCIIplus
        STR     R2,R1,#0
        BRnzp   BtAHundreds
BtANeg
        LD      R2,ASCIIminus
        STR     R2,R1,#0
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1        ; R0 = |value|
BtAHundreds
        LD      R2,ASCIIoffset  ; R2 = '0'
        LD      R3,Neg100
BtALoop100
        ADD     R0,R0,R3        ; R0 -= 100
        BRn     BtAEnd100
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        BRnzp   BtALoop100
BtAEnd100
        STR     R2,R1,#1        ; Store hundreds ASCII
        LD      R3,Pos100
        ADD     R0,R0,R3        ; Correct for overshoot
        LD      R2,ASCIIoffset
        LD      R3,Neg10
BtALoop10
        ADD     R0,R0,R3
        BRn     BtAEnd10
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        BRnzp   BtALoop10
BtAEnd10
        STR     R2,R1,#2
        ADD     R0,R0,#10       ; Correct for overshoot
        LD      R2,ASCIIoffset
        ADD     R2,R2,R0        ; Ones digit
        STR     R2,R1,#3
        ; Null-terminate
        AND     R2,R2,#0
        STR     R2,R1,#4
        LD      R7,BtASaveR7
        RET
BtASaveR7 .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; Data Section
; ============================================================

; --- Stack ---
StackMax   .BLKW  5             ; 5 slots (x3FFB to x3FFF)
StackBase  .FILL  x0000         ; Sentinel (stack empty when R6 = addr+1)

; --- ASCII Buffer (4 chars + null for display, up to 3 digits for input) ---
ASCIIBUFF  .BLKW  6

; --- Prompt ---
PromptMsg  .FILL  x000A
           .STRINGZ "Enter a command: "

; --- Error messages ---
OverflowMsg  .FILL x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Stack is Full."
UnderflowMsg .FILL x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Too Few Values on the Stack."
RangeErrMsg  .FILL x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Number is out of range."
DivZeroMsg   .FILL x000A
             .STRINGZ "Error: Division by zero."
TooManyDigits .FILL x000A
              .STRINGZ "Too many digits"

; --- Constants for dispatch ---
NegX       .FILL  xFFA8         ; -'X' = -x58
NegC       .FILL  xFFBD         ; -'C' = -x43
NegPlus    .FILL  xFFD5         ; -'+' = -x2B
NegMult    .FILL  xFFD6         ; -'*' = -x2A
NegPercent .FILL  xFFDB         ; -'%' = -x25
NegAt      .FILL  xFFC0         ; -'@' = -x40
NegN       .FILL  xFF92         ; -'n' = -x6E
NegD       .FILL  xFFBC         ; -'D' = -x44
NegMinus   .FILL  xFFD3         ; -'-' = -x2D
NegMinus2  .FILL  xFFD3         ; -'-' (second copy for PushValue)
NegZero    .FILL  xFFD0         ; -'0' = -x30
NegNine    .FILL  xFFC7         ; -'9' = -x39
NegE       .FILL  xFF9B         ; -'e' = -x65
NegG       .FILL  xFF99         ; -'g' = -x67

; --- Range check constants ---
Pos999     .FILL  #999
Neg999     .FILL  #-999

; --- ASCII conversion constants ---
NegASCIIOffset .FILL xFFD0      ; -x30
ASCIIplus      .FILL x002B
ASCIIminus     .FILL x002D
ASCIIoffset    .FILL x0030
Neg100         .FILL xFF9C      ; -100
Pos100         .FILL x0064      ; +100
Neg10          .FILL xFFF6      ; -10

; --- Lookup tables for ASCIItoBinary ---
LookUp10   .FILL  #0
           .FILL  #10
           .FILL  #20
           .FILL  #30
           .FILL  #40
           .FILL  #50
           .FILL  #60
           .FILL  #70
           .FILL  #80
           .FILL  #90

LookUp100  .FILL  #0
           .FILL  #100
           .FILL  #200
           .FILL  #300
           .FILL  #400
           .FILL  #500
           .FILL  #600
           .FILL  #700
           .FILL  #800
           .FILL  #900

MaxDigits  .FILL  #3

        .END

turns.content:
I see several issues I need to fix:

1. **RangeCheck**: The first `BRp RCBadRange` line is wrong — it's a leftover comment artifact. Let me clean it up properly.
2. **RangeCheck**: R0 isn't saved/restored (the `RCSaveR0` is never populated).
3. **Stack layout**: The `StackMax`/`StackBase` are defined late in the data section — the stack should be at specific addresses. Let me restructure by placing the stack at a known high address region.
4. **BinarytoASCII in OpDisplay**: calls `BinarytoASCII` which clobbers R0, but we already stored it to `DispSaveVal`, we just need to restore it properly.
5. **`ADD R3,R0,#-13`** — LC-3 only allows 5-bit signed immediates (-16 to 15), so -13 = #-13 is fine (within range).

Let me rewrite the file cleanly:

tool_calls.name: write
tool_calls.arguments:
{"content": "; ============================================================
; LC-3 Stack-Based Calculator
; Commands: X (exit), C (clear), + (add), * (mult),
;           % (mod), @ (XOR), neg (negate), D (display),
;           digits (push integer, supports sign '-')
;
; Stack grows downward. R6 = stack pointer.
; PUSH: R6--, M[R6] = R0  (full check against StackMax)
; POP:  R0 = M[R6], R6++  (empty check against StackBase+1)
; R5=0 success, R5=1 failure
; Results must be in [-999, 999]; else error + stack restored.
; ============================================================
        .ORIG   x3000

; ============================================================
; INITIALIZATION
; ============================================================
Init
        LEA     R6,StackBase        ; R6 <- address of StackBase
        ADD     R6,R6,#1            ; R6 = StackBase+1 (empty; stack is above StackBase)

; ============================================================
; MAIN LOOP
; ============================================================
NewCommand
        LEA     R0,PromptMsg
        PUTS
        GETC
        OUT

Test
        ; Check 'X'
        LD      R1,NegX
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     ExitProg

        ; Check 'C'
        LD      R1,NegC
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpClear

        ; Check '+'
        LD      R1,NegPlus
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpAdd

        ; Check '*'
        LD      R1,NegMult
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpMult

        ; Check '%'
        LD      R1,NegPct
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpMod

        ; Check '@'
        LD      R1,NegAt
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpXOR

        ; Check 'n' (beginning of "neg")
        LD      R1,NegN
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     ReadNeg

        ; Check 'D'
        LD      R1,NegD
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     OpDisplay

        ; Check '-' (negative number)
        LD      R1,NegMinus
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRz     PushValue

        ; Check digit '0'-'9'
        LD      R1,NegZero          ; R1 = -0x30
        ADD     R2,R0,R1            ; R2 = char - '0'
        BRn     BadCmd              ; < '0', not a digit
        LD      R1,NegNine          ; R1 = -0x39
        ADD     R2,R0,R1            ; R2 = char - '9'
        BRp     BadCmd              ; > '9', not a digit
        BRnzp   PushValue

BadCmd
        BRnzp   NewCommand

; ============================================================
; ReadNeg: consume 'e','g' to complete "neg"
; ============================================================
ReadNeg
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R1,NegE
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRnp    BadCmd2
        GETC
        OUT
        LD      R1,NegG
        ADD     R1,R1,R0
        BRnp    BadCmd2
        BRnzp   OpNeg
BadCmd2
        BRnzp   NewCommand

ExitProg
        HALT

; ============================================================
; OpClear - Reset stack
; ============================================================
OpClear
        LEA     R6,StackBase
        ADD     R6,R6,#1
        BRnzp   NewCommand

; ============================================================
; OpAdd - a + b
; Pop first (a, top), pop second (b), compute b+a
; ============================================================
OpAdd
        ST      R7,SaveR7_A
        JSR     POP                 ; a -> R0
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpAdd_Exit
        ADD     R1,R0,#0            ; R1 = a
        JSR     POP                 ; b -> R0
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpAdd_R1            ; restore a
        ADD     R0,R0,R1            ; result = b + a
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpAdd_R2            ; out of range: restore both
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7_A
        BRnzp   NewCommand
OpAdd_R2
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1           ; restore b slot
OpAdd_R1
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1           ; restore a slot
OpAdd_Exit
        LD      R7,SaveR7_A
        BRnzp   NewCommand
SaveR7_A  .FILL  x0000

; ============================================================
; OpMult - a * b  (repeated addition, handles sign)
; Pop first (a, top), pop second (b), compute b*a
; ============================================================
OpMult
        ST      R7,SaveR7_M
        AND     R3,R3,#0            ; R3 = sign flag (0=positive result)
        JSR     POP                 ; a (first/top) -> R0
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpMult_Exit
        ADD     R1,R0,#0            ; R1 = a (multiplicand)
        JSR     POP                 ; b (second) -> R0 (multiplier)
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpMult_R1
        ADD     R2,R0,#0            ; R2 = b, test sign
        BRzp    Mult_PosB
        ADD     R3,R3,#1            ; b negative: toggle sign flag
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1            ; R2 = |b|
Mult_PosB
        ; Also handle negative a
        ADD     R1,R1,#0
        BRzp    Mult_PosA
        NOT     R3,R3               ; flip low bit: XOR sign flag with 1
        AND     R3,R3,#1            ; keep only bit 0
        NOT     R1,R1
        ADD     R1,R1,#1            ; R1 = |a|
Mult_PosA
        AND     R0,R0,#0            ; Clear product
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     Mult_Done           ; multiplier zero -> product 0
Mult_Loop
        ADD     R0,R0,R1
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
        BRp     Mult_Loop
Mult_Done
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpMult_R2
        ; Apply sign
        ADD     R3,R3,#0
        BRz     Mult_Push
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
Mult_Push
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7_M
        BRnzp   NewCommand
OpMult_R2
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpMult_R1
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpMult_Exit
        LD      R7,SaveR7_M
        BRnzp   NewCommand
SaveR7_M  .FILL  x0000

; ============================================================
; OpMod - a % b  (remainder, sign follows dividend a)
; Pop a (top/dividend), pop b (divisor)
; Compute |a| mod |b|, restore sign of a
; ============================================================
OpMod
        ST      R7,SaveR7_Mo
        JSR     POP                 ; a (dividend, top) -> R0
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpMod_Exit
        ADD     R1,R0,#0            ; R1 = a
        JSR     POP                 ; b (divisor) -> R0
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpMod_R1
        ADD     R2,R0,#0            ; R2 = b

        ; Division by zero check
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     OpMod_Zero

        ; Track sign (result sign = sign of dividend a)
        AND     R3,R3,#0            ; R3 = result sign flag
        ADD     R4,R1,#0            ; R4 = a
        BRzp    Mod_APos
        ADD     R3,R3,#1
        NOT     R4,R4
        ADD     R4,R4,#1            ; R4 = |a|
Mod_APos
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRzp    Mod_BPos
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1            ; R2 = |b|
Mod_BPos
        ; Repeated subtraction: R4 mod R2
Mod_Loop
        NOT     R0,R2
        ADD     R0,R0,#1            ; R0 = -|b|
        ADD     R0,R4,R0            ; R0 = |a| - |b|
        BRn     Mod_Done
        ADD     R4,R0,#0
        BRnzp   Mod_Loop
Mod_Done
        ADD     R0,R4,#0            ; R0 = remainder (non-negative)
        ADD     R3,R3,#0
        BRz     Mod_SignOK
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1            ; Negate (sign of a)
Mod_SignOK
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpMod_R2
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7_Mo
        BRnzp   NewCommand

OpMod_Zero
        LEA     R0,DivZeroMsg
        PUTS
        ; Both a and b were popped; restore them
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1           ; restore b
OpMod_R1_Goto                       ; (fall through into R1 restore)
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1           ; restore a
        LD      R7,SaveR7_Mo
        BRnzp   NewCommand

OpMod_R2
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1           ; restore b
OpMod_R1
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1           ; restore a
OpMod_Exit
        LD      R7,SaveR7_Mo
        BRnzp   NewCommand
SaveR7_Mo .FILL  x0000

; ============================================================
; OpXOR - a XOR b
; Pop a (top), pop b
; a XOR b = NOT( NOT(NOT_a AND b) AND NOT(a AND NOT_b) )
; ============================================================
OpXOR
        ST      R7,SaveR7_X
        JSR     POP                 ; a -> R0
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpXOR_Exit
        ADD     R1,R0,#0            ; R1 = a
        JSR     POP                 ; b -> R0
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpXOR_R1
        ADD     R2,R0,#0            ; R2 = b
        ; Compute XOR
        NOT     R3,R1               ; R3 = ~a
        AND     R3,R3,R2            ; R3 = ~a & b
        NOT     R4,R2               ; R4 = ~b
        AND     R4,R1,R4            ; R4 = a & ~b
        NOT     R3,R3               ; R3 = ~(~a & b)
        NOT     R4,R4               ; R4 = ~(a & ~b)
        AND     R0,R3,R4            ; R0 = ~(~a & b) & ~(a & ~b)
        NOT     R0,R0               ; R0 = a XOR b
        JSR     RangeCheck
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpXOR_R2
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7_X
        BRnzp   NewCommand
OpXOR_R2
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpXOR_R1
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1
OpXOR_Exit
        LD      R7,SaveR7_X
        BRnzp   NewCommand
SaveR7_X  .FILL  x0000

; ============================================================
; OpNeg - Negate top of stack
; ============================================================
OpNeg
        ST      R7,SaveR7_N
        JSR     POP
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpNeg_Exit
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
        JSR     PUSH
OpNeg_Exit
        LD      R7,SaveR7_N
        BRnzp   NewCommand
SaveR7_N  .FILL  x0000

; ============================================================
; OpDisplay - Display top of stack (without popping)
; ============================================================
OpDisplay
        ST      R7,SaveR7_D
        JSR     POP                 ; peek top -> R0
        ADD     R5,R5,#0
        BRp     OpDisp_Exit         ; empty
        ST      R0,DispVal          ; save value
        JSR     BinarytoASCII       ; fills ASCIIBUFF, clobbers R0
        LD      R0,NewlineChar
        OUT
        LEA     R0,ASCIIBUFF
        PUTS
        ; Restore the popped element back
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1           ; undo the POP (push back in place)
OpDisp_Exit
        LD      R7,SaveR7_D
        BRnzp   NewCommand
SaveR7_D  .FILL  x0000
DispVal   .FILL  x0000
NewlineChar .FILL x000A

; ============================================================
; PushValue - Read (remaining) digits of a number and push
; R0 = first character already echoed (digit or '-')
; ============================================================
PushValue
        ST      R7,SaveR7_PV
        LEA     R1,ASCIIBUFF
        LD      R2,MaxDigits        ; R2 = 3 (max digit characters)

        ; Check for leading '-'
        AND     R4,R4,#0            ; R4 = sign flag (0=positive)
        LD      R3,NegMinus
        ADD     R3,R3,R0
        BRnp    PV_NotMinus
        ADD     R4,R4,#1            ; Negative number
        GETC
        OUT                         ; Read first actual digit
PV_NotMinus
        ; Store current character (first digit)
        STR     R0,R1,#0
        ADD     R1,R1,#1
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1

PV_Loop
        GETC
        OUT
        ; Check for line terminator
        ADD     R3,R0,#-13          ; CR?
        BRz     PV_Good
        ADD     R3,R0,#-10          ; LF?
        BRz     PV_Good
        ; Check digit buffer full
        ADD     R2,R2,#0
        BRz     PV_TooLarge
        ; Store digit
        STR     R0,R1,#0
        ADD     R1,R1,#1
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1
        BRnzp   PV_Loop

PV_Good
        ; R1 = end pointer, compute length
        LEA     R2,ASCIIBUFF
        NOT     R2,R2
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        ADD     R1,R1,R2            ; R1 = digit count
        JSR     ASCIItoBinary       ; R0 = binary value
        ; Apply sign
        ADD     R4,R4,#0
        BRz     PV_Push
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1
PV_Push
        JSR     PUSH
        LD      R7,SaveR7_PV
        BRnzp   NewCommand

PV_TooLarge
        ; Flush remainder of line
PV_Flush
        GETC
        OUT
        ADD     R3,R0,#-13
        BRz     PV_FlushDone
        ADD     R3,R0,#-10
        BRnp    PV_Flush
PV_FlushDone
        LEA     R0,TooManyMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,SaveR7_PV
        BRnzp   NewCommand
SaveR7_PV .FILL  x0000

; ============================================================
; PUSH subroutine
; In:  R0 = value to push
; Out: R5 = 0 success, R5 = 1 overflow (prints error)
; Saves/restores R1
; ============================================================
PUSH
        ST      R7,PUSH_SaveR7
        ST      R1,PUSH_SaveR1
        ; Check overflow: if R6 == StackMax, full
        LEA     R1,StackMax
        NOT     R1,R1
        ADD     R1,R1,#1            ; R1 = -addr(StackMax)
        ADD     R1,R1,R6            ; R6 - StackMax
        BRz     PUSH_Overflow
        ADD     R6,R6,#-1           ; Decrement SP
        STR     R0,R6,#0            ; Store value
        LD      R1,PUSH_SaveR1
        LD      R7,PUSH_SaveR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        RET
PUSH_Overflow
        ST      R7,PUSH_SaveR7B
        LEA     R0,OverflowMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,PUSH_SaveR7B
        LD      R1,PUSH_SaveR1
        LD      R7,PUSH_SaveR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1
        RET
PUSH_SaveR7  .FILL x0000
PUSH_SaveR7B .FILL x0000
PUSH_SaveR1  .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; POP subroutine
; Out: R0 = popped value, R5 = 0 success, R5 = 1 underflow
; Saves/restores R1
; ============================================================
POP
        ST      R7,POP_SaveR7
        ST      R1,POP_SaveR1
        ; Check underflow: if R6 == StackBase+1 (== addr of StackBase +1), empty
        ; Equivalently: R6 - (StackBase + 1) == 0
        LEA     R1,StackBase
        ADD     R1,R1,#1            ; R1 = addr(StackBase) + 1
        NOT     R1,R1
        ADD     R1,R1,#1            ; R1 = -(addr(StackBase)+1)
        ADD     R1,R1,R6            ; R6 - (StackBase+1)
        BRz     POP_Underflow
        LDR     R0,R6,#0
        ADD     R6,R6,#1
        LD      R1,POP_SaveR1
        LD      R7,POP_SaveR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        RET
POP_Underflow
        ST      R7,POP_SaveR7B
        LEA     R0,UnderflowMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,POP_SaveR7B
        LD      R1,POP_SaveR1
        LD      R7,POP_SaveR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1
        RET
POP_SaveR7  .FILL x0000
POP_SaveR7B .FILL x0000
POP_SaveR1  .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; RangeCheck subroutine
; In:  R0 = value to check (preserved on return)
; Out: R5 = 0 if -999 <= R0 <= 999, else R5 = 1 (prints error)
; ============================================================
RangeCheck
        ST      R7,RC_SaveR7
        ST      R0,RC_SaveR0        ; Preserve R0
        ; Check R0 >= -999: compute R0 + 999; if negative, too small
        LD      R4,Pos999
        ADD     R3,R0,R4            ; R3 = R0 + 999
        BRn     RC_Bad
        ; Check R0 <= 999: compute 999 - R0 = 999 + (-R0)
        NOT     R3,R0
        ADD     R3,R3,#1            ; R3 = -R0
        LD      R4,Pos999
        ADD     R3,R4,R3            ; R3 = 999 - R0; if negative, R0 > 999
        BRn     RC_Bad
        ; In range
        LD      R0,RC_SaveR0
        LD      R7,RC_SaveR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        RET
RC_Bad
        ST      R7,RC_SaveR7B
        LEA     R0,RangeErrMsg
        PUTS
        LD      R7,RC_SaveR7B
        LD      R0,RC_SaveR0
        LD      R7,RC_SaveR7
        AND     R5,R5,#0
        ADD     R5,R5,#1
        RET
RC_SaveR7  .FILL x0000
RC_SaveR7B .FILL x0000
RC_SaveR0  .FILL x0000

; ============================================================
; ASCIItoBinary
; In:  ASCIIBUFF[0..R1-1] = ASCII digit characters
;      R1 = number of digits (1..3)
; Out: R0 = binary integer value
; ============================================================
ASCIItoBinary
        AND     R0,R0,#0
        ADD     R1,R1,#0
        BRz     AtoB_Done

        LD      R3,NegASCII         ; R3 = -0x30
        LEA     R2,ASCIIBUFF
        ADD     R2,R2,R1
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1           ; point to ones digit

        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3
        ADD     R0,R0,R4            ; ones contribution

        ADD     R1,R1,#-1
        BRz     AtoB_Done
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1           ; point to tens digit
        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3
        LEA     R5,LookUp10
        ADD     R5,R5,R4
        LDR     R4,R5,#0
        ADD     R0,R0,R4            ; tens contribution

        ADD     R1,R1,#-1
        BRz     AtoB_Done
        ADD     R2,R2,#-1           ; point to hundreds digit
        LDR     R4,R2,#0
        ADD     R4,R4,R3
        LEA     R5,LookUp100
        ADD     R5,R5,R4
        LDR     R4,R5,#0
        ADD     R0,R0,R4            ; hundreds contribution
AtoB_Done
        RET

; ============================================================
; BinarytoASCII
; In:  R0 = signed integer in [-999,999]
; Out: ASCIIBUFF = null-terminated ASCII string (sign + 3 digits)
; Clobbers R0,R1,R2,R3
; ============================================================
BinarytoASCII
        ST      R7,BtoA_SaveR7
        LEA     R1,ASCIIBUFF
        ADD     R0,R0,#0
        BRn     BtoA_Neg
        LD      R2,ASCIIPlus
        STR     R2,R1,#0
        BRnzp   BtoA_H
BtoA_Neg
        LD      R2,ASCIIMinus
        STR     R2,R1,#0
        NOT     R0,R0
        ADD     R0,R0,#1            ; R0 = |value|
BtoA_H
        LD      R2,ASCIIOffset      ; '0'
        LD      R3,Neg100
BtoA_H_Loop
        ADD     R0,R0,R3            ; subtract 100
        BRn     BtoA_H_End
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        BRnzp   BtoA_H_Loop
BtoA_H_End
        STR     R2,R1,#1            ; store hundreds digit
        LD      R3,Pos100
        ADD     R0,R0,R3            ; correct overshoot
        LD      R2,ASCIIOffset
        LD      R3,Neg10
BtoA_T_Loop
        ADD     R0,R0,R3
        BRn     BtoA_T_End
        ADD     R2,R2,#1
        BRnzp   BtoA_T_Loop
BtoA_T_End
        STR     R2,R1,#2            ; store tens digit
        ADD     R0,R0,#10           ; correct overshoot
        LD      R2,ASCIIOffset
        ADD     R2,R2,R0
        STR     R2,R1,#3            ; store ones digit
        AND     R2,R2,#0
        STR     R2,R1,#4            ; null terminator
        LD      R7,BtoA_SaveR7
        RET
BtoA_SaveR7 .FILL x0000

turns.content:
All constants are correct. Now let me think through a few critical logic issues:

**Issue 1: OpMult sign tracking** — Using `NOT R3,R3` then `AND R3,R3,#1` — but in LC-3, `#1` as a 5-bit immediate is `x01`, and AND with immediate AND R3,R3,#1 would clear all bits except bit 0. However, the LC-3 AND with an immediate zero-extends only 5 bits — AND #1 = AND x0001 — so that's actually correct for a sign bit.

Wait, actually `AND R3,R3,#1` in LC-3 with the 5-bit zero-extended immediate 1 (`00001`) will AND all 16 bits with `0000000000000001`, leaving only bit 0. That does implement XOR with 1 on the low bit. ✓

**Issue 2: OpDisplay** — I do `ADD R6,R6,#-1` to undo the POP. This puts R6 back to where the value was, and since the value is still there in memory (we only incremented R6), this is correct. ✓

**Issue 3: OpMod division-by-zero path** — When b==0, both a and b have been popped. The code does `ADD R6,R6,#-1` then falls through with another `-1`. But the flow uses `OpMod_R1_Goto` label followed by `ADD R6,R6,#-1`. Let me re-check that:

turns.content:
I see the issue — `OpMod_R1_Goto` is a label but not a branch target properly, and `OpMod_R2` / `OpMod_R1` labels used for stack restoration might conflict. Let me also check the multiplication sign flip — the `NOT R3,R3` approach is risky in LC-3 because NOT flips all 16 bits, making it `xFFFF` when R3 was 0 and 0 when R3 was 1. Then `AND R3,R3,#1` keeps only bit 0. That gives 1 when input was 0, and 0 when input was 1. That's correct XOR behavior.

But wait — in LC-3, the ADD/AND immediate is only 5 bits, sign-extended. `AND R3,R3,#1` → immediate = 5-bit `00001` = `x0001`. But LC-3 sign-extends 5-bit immediates, so `00001` stays `0x0001`. The result AND R3,R3,#0x0001... Actually, LC-3 AND with a 5-bit immediate: bit pattern `00001` sign-extended to 16 bits = `0x0001`. So `AND R3, R3, x0001` — keeps only bit 0. ✓

Let me also fix the `OpMod_R1_Goto` label issue (it's not really needed, the fall-through is fine, just clean up the label name), and verify the stack restoration logic for all ops:

{"command": "cd /tmp/isolated_workspace_1091 && python3 -c "
# Simulate PUSH/POP and stack boundary conditions
# Stack: BASE (empty sentinel) then 5 slots up
# StackBase = some address B, StackMax = B-5 (lower address = higher stack)
# empty: R6 = B+1 (one past StackBase -- wait this is confusing)

# From context/10.5.asm:
# BASE .FILL xC001 = -0x3FFF
# So addr(BASE) = 0x3FFF
# empty check: R6 + BASE_NEG + (-1) == 0 => R6 == 0x4000 ??? 
# 
# Wait, re-read 10.5.asm:
# POP: R1 = BASE = xC001 = -x3FFF
# R1 = R1 - 1 = -x4000
# R2 = R6 + R1 = R6 - x4000
# BRz -> empty if R6 == x4000 ??? That doesn't match...
# 
# Actually xC001 = -0x3FFF in 2s complement (16-bit)
# R1 = xC001 (value of BASE constant) = -0x3FFF
# R1 = R1 + (-1) = -0x4000
# R2 = R6 + R1 = R6 - 0x4000
# BRz if R6 == 0x4000... but stack is at 0x3FFF
# 
# Wait, that can't be right. Let me re-read:
# BASE .FILL xC001 -- this is a value that when added to something useful...
# addr of BASE label itself = some address in program
# xC001 in the reference is: the NEGATIVE of the stack BASE ADDRESS
# -addr(BASE_of_stack) = xC001 means addr(BASE_of_stack) = 0x3FFF
# But it's loaded with LD R1,BASE, meaning R1 gets the VALUE stored at label BASE,
# which is xC001 = -0x3FFF.
# Then R1 = R1 + (-1) = -0x3FFF - 1 = -0x4000
# R2 = R6 + R1 = R6 - 0x4000
# BRz if R6 == 0x4000 -- empty if R6 starts at 0x4000? That's one past 0x3FFF...
# Actually the stack BASE (empty level) = 0x4000?? No...
# 
# Hmm. Looking at the 10.22 reference more carefully:
# 'StackBase .FILL x0000' -- that's data memory, not the literal stack address
# 'StackMax .BLKW 9' is the actual stack space
# POP checks: LEA R0,StackBase; NOT; ADD #1; ADD R6; BRz Underflow
# => checks R6 == addr(StackBase)
# With 10.22: R6 starts at addr(StackBase)+1 = one past the sentinel
# PUSH checks: LEA R1,StackMax; NOT; ADD #1; ADD R6; BRz => R6 == addr(StackMax)
# 
# So in 10.22-style (our style):
# StackMax (lowest addr) ... StackBase (highest addr, sentinel)
# empty: R6 == addr(StackBase)+1 (one ABOVE StackBase in memory)
# Wait, but then PUSH decrements R6, so pushing moves R6 down toward StackMax
# full: R6 == addr(StackMax)
#
# My implementation: 
# Init: R6 = addr(StackBase) + 1
# POP empty check: R6 == addr(StackBase)+1
#   LEA R1,StackBase; ADD R1,R1,#1; NOT R1,R1; ADD R1,R1,#1; ADD R1,R1,R6; BRz
# PUSH full check: R6 == addr(StackMax)
#   LEA R1,StackMax; NOT R1,R1; ADD R1,R1,#1; ADD R1,R1,R6; BRz

# Let's verify: if StackMax is at address M and StackBase at address B = M+5
# (since BLKW 5 gives 5 cells M, M+1, M+2, M+3, M+4 and then StackBase at M+5)
# empty: R6 = B+1 = M+6
# after 1 push: R6 = M+5 = B (the sentinel slot)
# Wait that means we're storing INTO StackBase's slot!

# Let me reconsider. StackBase should be ABOVE all stack slots.
# StackMax ... (5 cells) ... StackBase
# Addresses: StackMax=M, M+1, M+2, M+3, M+4, StackBase=M+5
# Empty: R6 = M+5+1 = M+6 = addr(StackBase)+1
# But then stack slots are M+5 down to M+1 (5 slots), plus M+4 is StackMax+4...
# 
# PUSH: R6-- then store at R6
# If R6 starts at M+6 (empty), first push: R6=M+5 (StackBase slot!) -- bad!
#
# Correct init should be R6 = addr(StackBase) itself, NOT +1
# Then:
# empty check: R6 == addr(StackBase)
# full check: R6 == addr(StackMax)
# First push: R6 = addr(StackBase)-1 = M+4 (last actual slot) ✓

print('Stack layout analysis:')
print('If init R6 = addr(StackBase), then:')
print('  empty check: R6 == StackBase (R6 - StackBase == 0)')
print('  After 1 push: R6 = StackBase-1 = StackMax+4 (using slot StackMax+4)')
print('  After 5 pushes: R6 = StackBase-5 = StackMax (full)')
print('  full check: R6 == StackMax ✓')
print()
print('In 10.22 reference:')
print('  StackMax .BLKW 9 -- 9 cells')
print('  StackBase .FILL x0000 -- sentinel at StackMax+9')
print('  Init: LEA R6,StackBase; ADD R6,R6,#1')
print('  This sets R6 = addr(StackBase)+1')
print('  Empty check: LEA R0,StackBase; NOT; +1; +R6; BRz')
print('  => checks R6 - addr(StackBase) == 0, i.e., R6 == addr(StackBase)')
print('  But R6 was set to addr(StackBase)+1...')
print('  That means R6 != addr(StackBase) on init -> NOT empty initially -> BUG?')
print()
print('Oh wait -- 10.22 init LEA+ADD#1 sets R6=addr(StackBase)+1')
print('POP: LEA R0,StackBase -> R0=addr(StackBase), NOT+1 -> -addr(StackBase)')
print('     ADD R0,R0,R6 = R6 - addr(StackBase)')
print('     If R6=addr(StackBase)+1: result=+1, BRz does NOT trigger -> stack not empty')
print('     After 1 pop of empty+1 init: we read addr(StackBase)+1, move R6 to +2...')
print()
print('Hmm - that seems wrong. Let me re-read 10.24 (the other POP ref):')
print('It uses LD R0,StackBase and StackBase .FILL x0000 (sentinel)')
print('Actually in 10.22: ADD R6,StackBase = the ADDRESS of the sentinel')
print('Empty when R6 points AT the StackBase sentinel')
print('Not one past -- I think the +1 in init is WRONG or I misread something')
"
"}

tool_calls.name: exec
tool_calls.arguments:
{"command": "cd /tmp/isolated_workspace_1091 && python3 -c "
# Let me carefully re-read 10.22 init and 10.22 POP
# 10.22:
# Init: LEA R6,StackBase; ADD R6,R6,#1
# This is the MAIN program init from 10.22 (combined PUSH/POP)
# But here StackBase is at the END of data (after StackMax .BLKW 9)
# If StackBase is at addr B, StackMax is at B-9
# LEA R6,StackBase -> R6=B
# ADD R6,R6,#1 -> R6=B+1
# 
# POP check: LEA R0,StackBase -> R0=B
#            NOT R0,R0 -> R0=-B-1
#            ADD R0,R0,#1 -> R0=-B
#            ADD R0,R0,R6 -> R0=R6-B
#            BRz if R6==B
# But R6 starts at B+1, so R6-B=1 -> NOT zero -> POP doesn't detect empty!
# 
# 10.27 (OpClear):
# LEA R6,StackBase; ADD R6,R6,#1
# This is the same init.
#
# Wait -- let me look at this from the 10.23 reference (PushValue):
# It references NewCommand which is from 10.22:
# The init sets R6=StackBase+1
# 10.22's POP checks R6 == StackBase (i.e., R6-StackBase==0)
# But stack is initialized to StackBase+1...
#
# OH. I think I finally understand.
# The stack grows DOWN from StackBase toward StackMax.
# StackBase is the BOTTOM (lowest-addressed end) and the empty state is R6=StackBase+1?
# No wait, that still doesn't work.
#
# Let me think about it differently with concrete addresses.
# Suppose StackBase is at address 0x1000, StackMax (9 words) starts at 0x0FF7.
# Wait, BLKW 9 comes BEFORE StackBase:
#   StackMax at 0x1000? No, LEA labels work sequentially.
#   If StackMax .BLKW 9 is assembled first, StackMax = some_addr
#   StackBase = StackMax + 9
# 
# Actually in memory layout, labels appear in order from lower to higher addresses.
# .ORIG x3000 + code + data:
#   ...
#   StackMax .BLKW 9   -> addresses M, M+1, ..., M+8
#   StackBase .FILL x0 -> address M+9
#
# Init: R6 = addr(StackBase) + 1 = M+10
# Empty check: R6 == addr(StackBase) = M+9
# R6=M+10: diff = +1, NOT zero -> not empty ✓ (but we haven't pushed anything!)
# 
# After PUSH: R6-- -> R6=M+9 -> STR R0,R6,#0 stores at M+9 (StackBase location)
# Hmm, storing into sentinel location.
#
# Actually wait - the stack grows UPWARD from lower to higher address here?
# No... Let me check 10.5.asm more carefully.
# BASE = xC001 = -0x3FFF, so addr(BASE) = 0x3FFF
# MAX  = xC005 = -0x3FFB, so addr(MAX) = 0x3FFB  (0x3FFB < 0x3FFF)
# So MAX is at a LOWER address than BASE.
# Stack grows from 0x3FFF downward toward 0x3FFB.
# empty: R6 == 0x3FFF+1 = 0x4000 ???
# 
# Re-reading 10.5 POP:
#   LD R1,BASE -> R1 = xC001 = -0x3FFF 
#   ADD R1,R1,#-1 -> R1 = xC001-1 = xC000 = -0x4000
#   ADD R2,R6,R1 -> R2 = R6 - 0x4000
#   BRz if R6 == 0x4000 -> empty
# So empty state R6 = 0x4000
# After one PUSH: R6=0x3FFF, stores at 0x3FFF
# After 5 pushes: R6=0x3FFB = MAX -> full check
# MAX check: LD R1,MAX=-0x3FFB; ADD R2,R6,R1=R6-0x3FFB; BRz if R6=0x3FFB ✓
#
# So in 10.5: the stack EMPTY state has R6 one ABOVE the actual stack region.
# Stack region: 0x3FFB to 0x3FFF (5 cells from MAX to BASE)
# BASE (addr 0x3FFF) is the TOPMOST cell (first one to be written on first push)
# MAX (addr 0x3FFB) is the lowest cell (last to be written when full)
# Empty: R6 = 0x4000 (one above BASE)
#
# Now in 10.22 style:
# StackMax .BLKW 9 (9 cells)
# StackBase .FILL x0  (sentinel at StackBase = StackMax+9)
# Init: R6 = addr(StackBase)+1
# Empty: R6 == addr(StackBase)+1 (not addr(StackBase)!)
# But POP checks R6 == addr(StackBase)... 
# WAIT. Let me reread 10.22 POP code:
# 'BRz fail_exit' when R6 == addr(StackBase)
# But if empty means R6 = addr(StackBase)+1 and we set it that way...
# ...then empty detection triggers ONLY when R6=addr(StackBase)?
# That would be WRONG (one-off error).
#
# I think 10.22 has a deliberate +1 offset. Let me reconsider:
# Maybe in 10.22, StackBase is the LAST slot (data is stored there), 
# and the sentinel is one above it.
# Stack slots: StackMax(M), M+1, ..., M+8, StackBase(M+9)
# Empty: R6 = M+10 = addr(StackBase)+1 (R6 points ONE PAST the last valid slot)
# First push: R6-- -> R6=M+9=addr(StackBase) -> store there -> uses StackBase slot
# That uses the sentinel slot as valid data location!
# 
# I think the textbook's 10.22 uses 10-slot stack (9 BLKW slots + 1 StackBase slot)
# and StackBase itself can be overwritten?? That seems odd.
# 
# OR: maybe the init should be LEA R6,StackBase (WITHOUT the +1)
# and 10.22 has a typo/discrepancy between the init shown in context 10.22 
# and the actual figure.
#
# The most consistent interpretation for MY implementation:
# StackMax .BLKW 5 = 5 slots
# StackBase .FILL = sentinel (not used for data)
# Empty: R6 = addr(StackBase) [pointing at sentinel, no valid data below]
# PUSH: decrement R6 to addr(StackBase)-1 = last slot of StackMax region
# Full: R6 == addr(StackMax)
# POP empty check: R6 == addr(StackBase)
# Init: R6 = addr(StackBase)
print('Corrected stack model:')
print('Init: R6 = addr(StackBase) directly (no +1)')
print('Empty: R6 == addr(StackBase)')
print('POP empty check: LEA R1,StackBase; -R1; ADD R6; BRz -> underflow')
print('PUSH full check: LEA R1,StackMax; -R1; ADD R6; BRz -> overflow')
print('First PUSH: R6-- = addr(StackBase)-1 = addr(StackMax)+4 ✓')
print('5th PUSH: R6 = addr(StackMax) -> next PUSH catches full ✓')
"
"}
